Its powers were first revealed in the Fish-Man Island Arc , but the name of the fruit was not given until the Zou Arc . Etymology Kame ( 亀 ) is the Japanese word for "turtle." Strengths and Weaknesses The primary ability this fruit grants is the ability to transform into a turtle at will. This turtle has a nearly-impenetrable protective shell (hard as diamond, according to Pekoms), being able to protect against bullets and collapsing buildings. However, being a turtle, the user will become naturally slower, and the transformation also causes the user's clothes to slip off. Also, this fruit does not offer many offensive capabilities, so Pekoms only uses this fruit to protect himself from attacks. Users of this fruit are also subject to the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Pekoms transforms into a turtle when he comes under attack in order to shield himself. However, when he transforms, his clothes slip off due to his body entering a shell, forcing him to fit them back on when he transforms back. He was also quick enough to activate his ability in time to block a majority of gunshots from Capone Bege 's attacks . In combat, Pekoms pairs this ability with other fighting techniques, such as martial arts and Electro .
The user touches a target and later redirects thrown objects toward that mark.
The user rams an opponent with a shield to break their stance.

The Nui Nui no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that grants the user the ability to stitch things together and un-stitch them like if nothing had happened. It was eaten by Leo . Etymology Nui ( 縫 ) means "to sew" or "to stitch" in Japanese. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, it is called the Stitch-Stitch Power . Appearance The Nui Nui no Mi is a spherical fruit divided in multiple segments (similar to an orange ), each of a different color, that are bound together by stitch-like growths. Its stem resembles a metallic sewing needle piercing through the fruit's main body, with a light green portion growing through the needle's eye in a manner similar to a thread of cloth. Strengths and Weaknesses This fruit allows the user to stitch objects to any surface. This makes it useful for restraining people, pinning them to the ground, to a wall or even other nearby individuals. The user is also able to undo the stitches of the object with ease, freeing it from their power. In the anime, other dwarves were also seen undoing the stitches. The stitches do not harm its victim and do not appear to physically puncture the object. The stitches are strong enough to hold Robin down, and Leo was able to stitch objects as large as Marine battleships together to prevent them from pursuing the Straw Hat Pirates . The latter proves that the ability can be used even on steel, implying that the hardness of an object does not hinder the sewing process. This fruit does not have any known specific weakness besides the general Devil Fruit weaknesses. However, it seems that a sewing needle is needed for the ability to actually work as Leo has yet to be shown using his fruit without one. Usage Leo uses this power to stitch and hold down his targets. He also uses this ability to support objects during earthquakes, to make sure they do not fall. It can also be used to reattach severed limbs, although irregular wound edges may compromise its efficiency and it will not heal the wound. Techniques Haute Couture: Patchwork ( 高級仕立 パッチ★ワーク , Ōto Kuchūru: Pacchi Wāku , literally meaning "High Class Sewing: Patchwork") : Leo stitches several objects or persons to his opponent. Then, by pulling the thread with his superhuman strength, he causes all stitched objects to converge towards his opponent, crashing into them with great force. It was first seen used against Giolla , stitching all of her comrades and subordinates around her. Haute Couture is French for "High Sewing" and refers to the practice of creating custom-fitted clothing.

Usopp Tactics Ginga Pachinko Kabuto Kuro Kabuto The slingshot Ginga Pachinko is Usopp 's first signature weapon. Usage Despite it being considered a child's toy, the slingshot in Usopp's hands can prove just as deadly as common firearms. As a matter of fact, it is the deceptive nature of the slingshot that often gives Usopp an advantage over opponents who do not think otherwise of him. In other words, the slingshot is the perfect weapon for a liar such as Usopp. Combined together with Usopp's sniper skills, it is a handy weapon. Since he was young, Usopp has developed a number of techniques involving his slingshot, mostly by firing different kinds of ammo. Not all of them are dangerous, but the majority of them are useful some way or another, even fatal if aimed properly. The majority of the ammo he shoots are named Boshi ( 星 , Boshi , literally meaning "Star") . Sometimes, he adds the word Hissatsu ( 必殺 , Hissatsu , literally meaning "Sure-Kill", translated as Death Blow , Killer , and other such words in the VIZ manga, My Special in the 4Kids dub and Special Attack in the Funimation adaptations and often in the VIZ manga) as he shouts the ammo's name and fires it to apparently make the attack sound more dangerous. He would also use the shouted out names as bluffs, saying one thing, but shooting something different. As Sogeking, Usopp's slingshot attacks are in English in order to disguise them. However, due to his new persona and Kabuto weapon, Usopp no longer uses this regular slingshot, but does occasionally use the same ammunition. Attacks The ammo and techniques Usopp can use with his slingshot are as follows: Namari Boshi ( 鉛星 , Namari Boshi , literally meaning "Lead Star") : Usopp fires a small lead ball bearing or pachinko ball from his slingshot. This was first seen being used as a named attack against the Black Cat Pirates as they were climbing up the slope to his village. In the English versions, this is called Lead Star . As Sogeking, this attack is called Metallic Star ( メタリック・スター , Metarikku Sutā ) . Kayaku Boshi ( 火薬星 , Kayaku Boshi , literally meaning "Gunpowder Star") : Usopp fires a highly explosive pellet filled with gunpowder. This was first seen being used to defeat Jango . According to Mr.5 , the gunpowder Usopp uses for this does not taste very good, which translates to Usopp using low quality gunpowder for the attack. In the English versions, this is called Exploding Star and sometimes Gunpowder Star . As Sogeking, this is called Sogeking Gunpowder Star ( そげキング 火薬星 , Sogekingu Ganpaudā Sutā ) . Sanren Kayaku Boshi ( 三連火薬星 , Sanren Kyaku Boshi , literally meaning "Triple Gunpowder Star") : Usopp fires three Kayaku Boshis at once. This was first used in the battle against Luffy. In the English adaptations, this is called Triple Exploding Star . Kayaku Hoshi Boshi ( 火薬星星 , Kyaku Hoshi Boshi , literally meaning "Gunpowder Star Star") : Basically the same as Kayaku Boshi but with Usopp jumping around dramatically while firing the shot. Usopp first displayed this move in an attempt to bring down Giant Jack. In the VIZ manga, this is called Exploding Stars . Rolling Kayaku Boshi ( 回転 火薬星 , Rōringu Kyaku Boshi , literally meaning "Rolling/Rotating Gunpowder Star") : Usopp rolls while firing off Kayaku Boshis. Usopp first displayed this move in an attempt to bring down Giant Jack. In the English versions, this is called Rolling Exploding Star . Tamago Boshi ( 卵星 , Tamago Boshi , literally meaning "Egg Star") : Usopp launches a rotten egg at the target's face in order to blind or distract them. This was first seen being used unsuccessfully against some of the Arlong Pirates in order to run away from them after he shot Arlong . This attack can be used to disable the enemy´s smell (as seen during his fight with Luffy ), and in the video game One Piece: Grand Adventure , it disorientates the target. In the English versions, this is called Egg Star . Shinsen Tamago Boshi ( 新鮮卵星 , Shinsen Tamago Boshi , literally meaning "Fresh Egg Star") : The same as Tamago Boshi only with a fresh egg instead of a rotten one. In the English versions, this is called Fresh Egg Star . This was first seen being used against Richie . While missing its target initially, the egg that was fired provided a path past the lion to the Going Merry , as Richie chased after the shot for a small and much needed meal. Kemuri Boshi ( 煙星 , Kemuri Boshi , literally meaning "Smoke Star") : Usopp fires a smoke bomb to confuse his enemies. This was first seen being used against Arlong and his pirates in an attempt to escape from them. In the English versions, this is called Smoke Star . As Sogeking, this technique is called Sogeking Smoke Star ( そげキング 煙星 , Sogekingu Sumōku Sutā ) . Kaen Boshi ( 火炎星 , Kaen Boshi , literally meaning "Flame Star") : Usopp fires a flame pellet. The damage of this attack can be increased by previously covering the target with flammable substance, like throwing alcohol bottles at them. This was first seen being used at Chew . If combined with a great amount of gas, it can unleash a big explosion; Usopp used a Breath Dial to perform this against Luffy. This is occasionally called Fire Star , Flame Star , and Flaming Star in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations and Hot Salsa Star in the 4Kids dub. Sometimes in the Funimation adaptations, it is referred to as Exploding Star . Tokusei Tabasco Boshi ( 特製タバスコ星 , Tokusei Tobasuko Boshi , literally meaning "Deluxe Tabasco Star") : Usopp fires a pellet containing Tabasco sauce either at the target's eyes or mouth. This was first seen being used against Mr.5 , with Usopp yelling the name Kayaku Boshi in order to disguise the attack. This is called Deluxe Pepper Sauce Star in the VIZ manga, Hot Sauce Star in the 4Kids dub, and Special Tobasco Star in the Funimation adaptations. Akahebi Boshi ( 赤蛇星 , Akahebi Boshi , literally meaning "Red Snake Star") : Usopp fires a signal flare that creates a red smoke pillar to alert his friends to his location. This was first seen being used to signal the other Straw Hats after Vivi deduced the location of Crocodile's bomb during the events in Alubarna . This is called Red Serpent Star in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations and Unstoppable Red Snake Star in the 4Kids dub. Shuriken Ryuseigun ( 手裏剣流星群 , Shuriken Ryūseigun , literally meaning "Shuriken Meteoric Swarm") : Usopp fires a chain of several attached shuriken which then separate into a large spread of shuriken to cover a wider radius. This was first seen used during his fight with Luffy. In the VIZ manga and 4Kids and Funimation adaptations, this is called Shuriken Shooting Star . Sakuretsu Saboten Boshi ( 炸裂サボテン星 , Sakuretsu Saboten Boshi , literally meaning "Exploding Cactus Star") : Usopp fires a green pellet that explodes on impact and showers the victim with quills. This was first seen being used in Usopp's battle with Luffy. This is called Blasting Cactus Star in the VIZ manga and 4Kids and Funimation dubs and Bursting Cactus Star in the Funimation subs. Non-Canon Attacks Nemuri Boshi ( 眠り星 , Nemuri Boshi , literally meaning "Sleep Star") : A specialized pink pellet created by the combined intellects of Usopp and Chopper. The pellet is a sleeping drug that, when fired into an enemy's mouth, puts them immediately to sleep. This was first seen in the second anime special against Bayan 's choir. Kosho Boshi ( 胡椒星 , Koshō Boshi , literally meaning "Pepper Star") : Usopp fires a pellet filled with pepper at an enemy, causing them to sneeze uncontrollably. This was first seen being used against Luffy in their battle in the anime. The sneezing caused Luffy to constantly fall upon Usopp's caltrops. In the Funimation dub, this is called Pepper Star. Anime and Manga Differences In the manga , it was originally depicted as being colored brown per the Volume 5 cover. In the anime , it is depicted as being colored green. The manga later changed and followed the anime's coloring also as it later depicted the slingshot as green on the Volume 35 cover.
The user uses quick front kicks to keep an enemy away.
The user feels danger seconds before a weapon is fired.
The user senses nearby presences without using eyesight.

Black Leg Style Diable Jambe Ifrit Jambe Team Combinations Anime Only The Black Leg Style is a martial-arts style used by Sanji . It was created by Sanji, based on the style used by Zeff during his days as a pirate , and named after his epithet . Description The Black Leg Style was created by Sanji, based on the kicking techniques and philosophy passed down by Zeff, with a complete emphasis on kicks, repurposing the use of hands into acrobatics such as handstands to augment the force and range of kicks and to prevent the hands from being damaged during a battle, something that is disastrous to a chef like Sanji. Thus, it boasts a wide and impressive array of kicking techniques coupled with superior acrobatic skills, making it an extremely versatile martial art, able to effectively weave continuous and powerful attacks upon adversaries and overpowered numerous enemies at once with incredible efficiency. Over time Sanji has incorporated the ability to run on air and ignite his legs into the Black Leg Style, which are now the staple parts of his fighting technique that make the Black Leg Style highly mobile and destructive. Zeff's Techniques Zeff used a similar unnamed style with a similar philosophy of using his legs only to fight, while considering that a chef must never tarnish his hands to fight. Zeff is extremely skilled and a powerful master in this martial art. In his prime, Zeff was renowned for being able to crush boulders, dent steel and even draw blood from enemies using the immense power of his lethal kicks, earning himself the epithet "Red Leg" as his boots would be coated in the blood of his enemies. Even in his old age and with the loss of his original leg, Zeff is still capable of performing his kicking techniques which use the naming scheme of "Head Chef": Ryoricho Gisoku Kick ( 料理長義足キック , Ryōrichō Gisoku Kikku , literally meaning "Head Chef Peg-Leg Kick") : A powerful horizontal kick with the tip of his peg leg. In the VIZ manga, this is called Chef's Special Peg-Leg Kick . Ryoricho Drop ( 料理長ドロップ , Ryōrichō Doroppu , literally meaning "Head Chef Drop") : A kick from his peg leg done by jumping in the air. Zeff destroyed his ceiling in the process of using this technique. In the VIZ manga, this is simply called Chef Drop , and in One Piece Grand Adventure, which was dubbed by 4Kids Entertainment, it is called Head Chef Drop . Video Game-Only Techniques Zeff Pulled Pork Pound: Zeff quickly kicks the opponent directly in front of him with his peg leg. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventur e . Zeff Fork Swing : After using Zeff Pulled Pork Pound , Zeff swings his other leg towards his opponent for a powerful kick. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Rolling Dish : After using Zeff Fork Swing , Zeff spins in a circle directly into his opponents while kicking them repeatedly. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Turnover Kick : After using Zeff Fork Swing , Zeff backflips behind his opponent and launches them into the air with a powerful vertical kick. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Gut Stuffer : After using Zeff Pulled Prok Pound , Zeff slams both his chef hat and his leg towards his opponent for a powerful horizontal kick. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Chicken Flambe : Zeff launches his opponent into the air with a vertical kick. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Shrimp Summer : After using Zeff Chicken Flambe , Zeff kicks his opponent again with his other leg to launch them into the air again. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Tatty Trouncer : After using Zeff Shrimp Summer , Zeff jumps through the air diagonally while kicking his opponents repeatedly. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Shank Crank : After using Zeff Shrimp Summer, Zeff kicks his opponent 3 times in a row in the air before slamming them into the ground with a finishing kick. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Fish Fillet : After using Zeff Chicken Flambe , Zeff dashes towards his opponent incredibly quickly, creating an air shockwave, before landing 3 powerful horizontal kicks in a row. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Cooking Summer : Zeff jumps into the air and performs Zeff Chicken Flambe while airborn. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Knife Shot : Zeff jumps into the air and lands a powerful diagonal kick downards towards his opponent. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Frog Leg Kick : Zeff kicks his opponent vertically into the air with his leg, then uses his peg leg to launch them much higher into the air with a second kick. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Open Face Slam : Zeff charges up his peg leg before slamming it directly into his opponent's face. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Red Foot Pride : Zeff charges up his peg leg before using a powerful horizontal kick on his opponent. This is one of Zeff's Specialty Attacks in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Pegleg Shoot : Similar to Zeff Knife Shot , except that Zeff's diagonal kick is vertical instead. This is one of Zeff's Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Zeff Table Change : Zeff jumps closer to his opponent, then quickly spins his leg in a circle to kick them. Zeff Leg O' Wisdom : Zeff kicks his opponent 12 times in a row as a combo attack with his peg leg. This is one of Zeff's Super Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Red Foot Shock : Zeff creates a powerful red air shockwave with his peg leg that heavily resembles Rankyaku , only with a red aura. This is one of Zeff's Super Techniques in One Piece: Grand Adventure . Sanji's Techniques Sanji mastered the kicking technique passed down by Zeff and developed his own Black Leg Style. After joining the Straw Hat Pirates as their chef and gaining greater fighting experience from constantly battling powerful enemies such as infamous Pirates and Marines, Sanji has surpassed his mentor, developing more powerful variations of Zeff's techniques and inventing his own unique kicking techniques. Sanji can combine the power of his kicks with his other crewmates' respective strengths for devastating team combinations. Most of Sanji's attack names are French words for cuts of meat or various dishes, though he has recently employed the use of English words when naming his techniques. Before the Timeskip Collier ( 首肉 , Korie , literally meaning "Neck") : Sanji delivers a powerful kick to the opponent's neck. This was first seen as a named attack in a series of attacks against Kuroobi . Collier is the French term for neck meat. This is called Collier Coup in the VIZ manga and Trachea Turnover in the 4Kids dub, but in the Funimation adaptations, it keeps its original name. This attack forms part of the Collier, Épaule, Côtelette, Selle, Poitrine, Gigot, and Mouton Shot combo. Collier Shoot ( 首肉 シュート , Korie Shūto , literally meaning "Neck Shoot") : An enhanced version of Collier. Sanji jumps high into the air and delivers a powerful kick to the opponent's neck. This was first seen as a named attack against Momoo . During the Straw Hat's great Enies Lobby escape, Sanji also performed this move to block and deflect the cannonballs fired by the Buster Call warships at the Going Merry. This is called Collier Coup in the VIZ manga and Funimation dub, Collier Shoot in the Funimation subs (and sometimes the dub), and Trachea Turnover Surprise in the 4Kids dub. After the timeskip, Sanji was seen using Collier Shoot with Diable Jambe against Judge . Épaule ( 肩肉 , Epōru , literally meaning "Shoulder") : A downward kick targeting the opponent's shoulder, meant to knock them off balance or drive them into the ground. This attack, first seen used against Kuroobi, was named in a series of kicks against the fish-man. Epaule is the French word for shoulder. This is called Épaule Sprawl in the VIZ manga and Clavicle Schnitzel Kick Off in the 4Kids dub, but in the Funimation adaptations, it keeps its original name. This attack forms part of the Collier, Épaule, Côtelette, Selle, Poitrine, Gigot, and Mouton Shot combo. Épaule Shoot ( 肩肉 シュート , Epōru Shūto , literally meaning "Shoulder Meat Shoot") : An enhanced version of Épaule. Sanji jumps into the air and brings his heel down on the opponent's shoulder. It was first seen against a Giant Sandora Lizard in Arabasta , in combination with Luffy 's Gomu Gomu no Muchi and Zoro 's Tatsu Maki . This is called Épaul Sprawl in the VIZ manga, and Epaule Shoot or Epaule Shot in the Funimation adaptations. Côtelette ( 背肉 , Kōtoretto , literally meaning "Rib Meat") : Dropping down into a one-handed handstand position, Sanji kicks the opponent hard in the ribs. This attack is usually followed by Selle . This attack was first named in a series of kicks against Kuroobi. Côtelette is the French term for rib meat. This is called Côtelette Kabosh in the VIZ manga, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack was edited out of the 4Kids dub. This attack forms part of the Collier, Épaule, Côtelette, Selle, Poitrine, Gigot, and Mouton Shot combo. Selle ( 鞍下肉 , Seru , literally meaning "Lower Back Meat") : Commonly used as a combo attack with Côtelette. Using the momentum from the Côtelette attack, Sanji (still in a handstand) spins around in the opposite direction to deliver a fast kick to the opponent's lower back. This attack was first named in a series of kicks against Kuroobi. Selle is the French term for lower back meat. In the VIZ manga, this is called Sélle de Ouch , but in the Funimation adaptations, it keeps its original name. This attack was also edited out of the 4Kids dub. This attack forms part of the Collier, Épaule, Côtelette, Selle, Poitrine, Gigot, and Mouton Shot combo. Poitrine ( 胸肉 , Powatorīnu , literally meaning "Breast Meat") : A stabbing kick into the opponent's chest. This attack was named in a series of kicks against Kuroobi. Poitrine is the French term for breast meat. This is called Poitrine Treader in the VIZ manga and Barbequed Rib Buster in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Collier, Épaule, Côtelette, Selle, Poitrine, Gigot, and Mouton Shot combo. Poitrine Shoot ( 胸肉 シュート , Powatorīnu Shūto , literally meaning "Breast Meat Shoot") : An enhanced version of Poitrine in which Sanji aims a little higher and kicks up instead of down. This attack was used as a named move against Arlong in the manga and in the Strong World film against Shiki . In the VIZ Manga, this is called Poitrine Treader , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Gigot ( もも肉 , Jigō , literally meaning "Leg Meat") : Sanji runs toward the opponent delivers a sweeping kick to the kneecaps, with opponent either sent flying or slammed to the ground as a result. This attack was named in a series of kicks against Kuroobi. Gigot is the French term for leg meat. Also, the hiragana usually associated with the kanji for this attack, ももにく, can literally mean "ham", the thigh meat of a pig or a boar. This is called Gigot Charlie-le-Horse in the VIZ manga and Shish Kebab Back Smash in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Collier, Épaule, Côtelette, Selle, Poitrine, Gigot, and Mouton Shot combo. Mouton Shot ( 羊肉 ショット , Mūton Shotto , literally meaning "Sheep Meat Shot") : Sanji dashes forward and channels all his might into one leg to deliver a flurry of kicks so fast that that they seem to hit simultaneously, creating the illusion that Sanji's leg is s ng (this is not properly shown the first time in the anime), sending the target flying. Sanji usually aims at the opponent's midsection, or sometimes their head. This technique was first used to finish off Kuroobi after a long and devastating series of kicks. Mouton is French for "mutton", meaning cooked lamb meat. This is called Mouton Mallet in the VIZ manga, Leg o' Wham Drumstick Supreme in the 4Kids dub, and Mouton Shoot in the Funimation adaptations, with it being called Face Shot in it's first appearance. Reception ( 受付 , Resepushon ) : Sanji lifts one of his legs up high and hooks his foot around the opponent's neck, then uses that leg to smash the opponent's face into the ground. This attack is not named after a food, but rather the "reception" that one gets when entering a restaurant. Sanji first used this attack against one of Wapol 's soldiers when they attacked the Going Merry . This attack keeps its original name in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, but is called Open-Face Slamwich in the 4Kids dub. Anti-Manner Kick Course ( 反行儀 キックコース , Anchimanā Kikku Kōsu ) : One of Sanji's strongest single kick attacks. He delivers a massive kick in which he lifts one of his legs a full 180 degrees in the air. This attack is usually aimed at the very center of the opponent's torso (front or back) and is almost always enough to defeat any opponent with one strike. For much taller and gigantic targets, Sanji usually jumps to the desired height to execute the move. He once used this attack horizontally on Oars ' foot in order to unbalance the giant. This attack was able to bypass Big Pan 's lubricated skin (where almost nothing else has) dealing severe damage to the Wotan . This is called Bad Manners Kick Course in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, Frog-Leg Kick in the 4Kids dub, and keeps its original name in the Funimation dub of Unlimited Adventure . Sanji first used this move to rescue his comrades from a Bananawani . Basse Côte ( 肩ロース , Bāsu Kōto , literally meaning "Shoulder Roast") : A mid-air attack in which Sanji kicks one leg straight forward to attack the opponent while kicking the other one backwards to provide the necessary momentum. It was first used against Mr. 2 Bon Kurei . Basse côte is the French term for shoulder meat. This is called Basses Cote in the Funimation adaptations, Basses Côte Blast in the VIZ manga, and Shoulder Shank Crank in the 4Kids dub. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Longe ( 腰肉 , Ronju , literally meaning "Loin Meat") : A sweeping kick that usually targets the opponent's back. It is useful for sending an opponent flying in preparation for another kick. It was first used against Mr. 2. Longe is the French word for loin. This is called Longe Lasher in the VIZ manga and Hip Hip Puree in the 4Kids dub, but in the Funimation adaptations, it keeps its original name. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Tendron ( 後バラ肉 , Tandoron , literally meaning "Collar Meat") : A straight side kick targeting the opponent's collar bone. It was first used against Mr. 2. Tendron is the French term for the meat from the upper chest. This is called Tendron Tenderizer in the VIZ manga and Tenderoin Lash in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Flanchet ( 腹肉 , Furanshe , literally meaning "Stomach Meat") : A simple kick to the opponent's stomach. It was first used against Mr. 2. Flanchet is the French term for lower abdomen meat. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. This is called Flanchel Flayer in the VIZ manga, but in the Funimation adaptations, it keeps its original name. Flanchet Shoot ( 腹肉 シュート , Furanshe Shūto , literally meaning "Stomach Meat Shoot") : An enhanced version of Flanchet. Sanji delivers a spinning kick to the opponent's stomach It was first used as a named attack against a Lapahn . This is called Flanchel Flayer in the VIZ manga and Gut Stuffer in the 4Kids dub, but in the Funimation adaptations, it keeps its original name. Quasi ( 上部もも肉 , Kaji , literally meaning "Upper Thigh Meat") : Sanji flips over into a double-handed handstand, spins around, and kicks the opponent. This attack is usually followed by Queue . It was first used against Mr. 2. Quasi is the French term for upper leg meat. This is called Quasi Cracker in the VIZ manga and Butterloin Breaker in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Queue ( 尾肉 , Kū , literally meaning "Tail Meat") : Commonly used as a combo attack with Quasi . Sanji continues spinning with the momentum from Quasi and swings his other leg around to kick the opponent's tail bone. It was first used against Mr. 2. Queue ( [kø] ) is the French word for tail. This is called Queue Crippler in the VIZ Manga and Rump Roast Wrecker in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Cuisseau ( もも肉 , Kyuisō , literally meaning "Thigh Meat") : Often used in combination with Jarret . Sanji sends a hard kick to the opponent's thigh. This can be used to attack or block an incoming kick. It was first used against Mr. 2. Cuisseau is the French term for thigh meat. This is called Cuisseau Crusher in the VIZ manga and Turkey Tenderizer in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Cuisseau Shoot ( もも肉 シュート , Kyuisō Shūto , literally meaning "Thigh Meat Shoot") : An enhanced version of Cuisseau. A hard kick to the thigh that sends the opponent flying. This was first seen being used against Jabra . In the VIZ manga, this is called Cuisseau Crusher , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Jarret ( すね肉 , Jare , literally meaning "Shin Meat") : Often used in combination with Cuisseau . Sanji delivers a hard kick to the opponent's shin. This can be used to attack or block an incoming kick. It was first used against Mr. 2. Jarret is the French term for meat from the back of the knee. This is called Jarret Jarrer in the VIZ manga and Joint Jim-Jam in the 4Kids dub, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, Jarret, and Veau Shot combo. Veau Shot ( 仔牛肉 ショット , Vō Shotto , literally meaning "Veal Shot") : One of Sanji's strongest attacks. He leaps into the air and sweeps his leg forward to land a powerful kick to the center of the opponent's torso. It was first used as last resort against Mr. 2. The first time Sanji used this attack successfully, it had a delayed effect, damaging Mr. 2 several seconds after the impact. Despite being quite powerful, a strong defense like a blocking stance while using Tekkai can counter most of this technique's damage, as shown by Jabra. This is called Veau Shoot or Veau Shot in the Funimation adaptations, Veau Vengeance in the VIZ manga, and Prime Cut Kick Surprise in the 4Kids dub. Sanji performs this move as a finishing blow after using his combo of Basse Côte, Longe, Tendron, Flanchet, Quasi, Queue, Cuisseau, and Jarret. Concassé ( 粗砕 , Konkasse , literally meaning "Crush") : One of Sanji's signature and strongest attacks. Sanji leaps high into the air and starts flipping rapidly to gain speed, then brings the heel of his foot down on the opponent's head. This attack was first used to defeat Satori . Sanji can also perform an alternate version of this attack by balancing on one hand and spinning sideways rapidly before delivering a kick to his opponent's side. This variation was powerful enough to stun Blueno when he was using Tekkai. Concassé is the French term for the process of smashing ingredients such as peppercorns, grains, or nuts into small fragments. This is called Concasser in the Funimation dub of Strong World and Unlimited Adventure , Concasser Crush in the VIZ manga, and Coconut Crush in the 4Kids-dubbed video games, but in the Funimation dub, the technique keeps its original name. However, when Sanji first used the technique, it was called Delicious in the Funimation dub. Troisième Hachis ( 三級挽き肉 , Torowajiemu Asshi , literally meaning "Third-Rate Mincemeat") : Sanji performs a series of backflips towards his opponent before using his hands for one final push to launch himself forward and deliver several fast kicks. This was first seen being used against Hamburg in the Davy Back Fight . Hachis is the French culinary term for mincemeat. This is called Troisième Hachis Hash in the VIZ manga and Gyro Jostler in the 4Kids-dubbed video games, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Bouquetière Shot ( 木犀型斬 ショット , Bukuteiēru Shotto , literally meaning "Mixed Vegetables Shot") : Often used as the final blow in a series of attacks. Sanji lands on his hands underneath an opponent, then uses both hands to spring upwards and deliver a blow to the opponent's chin with both feet. This was first seen being used against Hamburg in the Davy Back Fight. Bouquetière is the French culinary term for a mix of fresh in-season vegetables. This is called Bouquetiere Beater in the VIZ manga and Double-Layer Cake Kick in the 4Kids-dubbed video games, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Slice Shoot ( 切肉 シュート , Suraisu Shūto , literally meaning "Sliced Meat Shoot") : A disarming attack in which Sanji runs forward, jumps into the air and kicks the opponent's hand to knock a weapon from it. This was first seen being used to knock Aokiji 's ice blade from his hand. In the VIZ manga, this is called Slice Shot and in the Funimation adaptations it is called Slice Shoot . Party Table Kick Course ( パーティーテーブルキックコース , Pātī Tēburu Kikku Kōsu ) : An attack for fighting against large groups. Sanji jumps into the air and lands on the head of one of the opponents in a handstand. He then spins around rapidly to deliver a kick to the face of anyone within range. After he runs out of targets, he dismounts and kicks the person he was spinning on. Sanji can also perform this move with a normal handstand on the ground. This attack was first seen in We Are! and was Sanji's first kicking technique shown in the series. However, it was not named until it was used against the Franky Family . This attack keeps its name in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, but is called Rolling Dish in the 4Kids-dubbed video games. Brochette ( 串焼き , Buroshietto , literally meaning "Skewer") : Sanji jumps over the top of his opponent and aims one leg directly downwards. He then drops downwards while spinning like a drill as he strikes his opponent with his extended leg. This was first seen being used against Jerry . A brochette is the French term for a skewer of cooked meat or fish. In the VIZ manga, this is called Brochette Blow , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Collier Frit ( 首肉 フリット , Korie Furitto , literally meaning "Fried Neck Meat") : Sanji leans forward while facing away from the opponent and launches a backwards kick upward into the opponent's throat. This attack is altered in the anime to a dual kick to defeat two opponents, with an added spin for extra damage. It was first displayed against a World Government Agent on the Puffing Tom (a pair of twins in the anime). Frites Assorties ( 揚げ物盛り合わせ , Furitto Asoruti , literally meaning "Fries Assortment") : An attack for use against several opponents. Sanji leans forward and kicks several opponents into the air. It was first used against three World Government Agents on the Puffing Tom. Escalope ( 薄切り肉のソテー , Esukaroppu , literally meaning "Thin Slices") : A jumping kick to the forehead. It was first used against Wanze . Escalope is a French culinary term for a thin boneless piece of meat. This is written as Éscalope in the VIZ manga. Œil ( 目 , Uiyu , literally meaning "Eye") : A straight kick to the opponent's eyes. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Œil is the French word for eye. In the VIZ manga, this is called Kick L'Oeil , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, Menton, and Parage Shot combo. Nez ( 鼻 , Ne , literally meaning "Nose") : A straight kick to the opponent's nose. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Nez is the French word for nose. In the VIZ manga, this is called Le Nez , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, Menton, and Parage Shot combo. Joue ( 頬 , Jū , literally meaning "Cheek") : A straight kick to the opponent's cheek. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Joue is the French word for cheek. In the VIZ manga, this is called La Joue , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, Menton, and Parage Shot combo. Joue Shoot ( ほほ肉 シュート , Jū Shūto , literally meaning "Cheek Shoot") : An enhanced version of Joue. Sanji leans forward and delivers a hard side kick to one of the opponent's cheeks. It was first used against Mr. 2. This is called Jou Baste in the VIZ manga, but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Bouche ( 口 , Būshu , literally meaning "Mouth") : A straight kick to the opponent's upper jaw. This attack targets the roof of their mouth, and thus can only be performed when the opponent's mouth is open. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Bouche is the French word for mouth. In the VIZ manga, this is called La Bouche , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, Menton, and Parage Shot combo. Dents ( 歯 , Dan , literally meaning "Teeth") : A straight kick to the opponent's teeth. Sanji hits both the upper and lower sets of teeth at the same time, and thus he can only use his attack when the opponent's mouth is closed. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Bouche is the French word for teeth. In the VIZ manga, this is called Les Dents , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, Menton, Parage Shot combo. Menton ( あご , Manton , literally meaning "Chin") : A straight kick to the opponent's chin. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Bouche is the French word for chin. In the VIZ manga, this is called Le Menton , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. This attack forms part of the Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, Menton, Parage Shot combo. Parage Shot ( 整形 ショット , Parāju Shotto , literally meaning "Cosmetic Surgery Shot") : Sanji delivers a barrage of kicks to each section of the opponent's face. These kicks are so powerful that they can alter the structure of the opponent's face, resulting in an effect similar to plastic surgery. It was first used against Wanze in a series of kicks to his face. Both victims of this attack ended up with "pretty boy" looks, though while Wanze despised it, Duval was thankful. Parage is a French verb meaning "to trim", which in culinary use means removing the inedible parts of meat or other food. In the VIZ manga, this was originally called Plastic Surgery Shot . Sanji performs this move as a finishing blow after using his combo of Œil, Nez, Joue, Bouche, Dents, and Menton. Santen Découpage ( 三点 切分 , Santen Dekupāju , literally meaning "Three Point Cut") : Sanji delivers three powerful near-simultaneous roundhouse kicks to the opponent's throat, chest and stomach, striking them with the entire length of his leg (the striking order is unclear, but according to the anime, the last hit is to the chest). This was first seen being used to finish off Wanze. Découpage is a French verb meaning "to cut up". In the VIZ manga, this is called Three-Point Decoupage and in the Funimation adaptations it is called Three-Spot Decoupage . Shishinabe Shoot ( 猪鍋シュート , Shishi Nabe Shūto , literally meaning "Boar Hot Pot Shoot") : Sanji attacks with a powerful flying sidekick. This attack was first used to break through a roof and reach the other Straw Hat Pirates on the tower of Enies Lobby. This is called Boar Soup Boot in the VIZ manga and Boar Stew Shoot in the Funimation adaptations. Deuxième Hachis ( 二級挽き肉 , Doujiemu Asshi , literally meaning "Second-Rate Mincemeat") : Sanji performs a running jump and kicks with both legs into an opponent's stomach. This was first seen being used against Jabra. In the VIZ manga, this is called Deuxième Hachis Hash , but it keeps its original name in the Funimation adaptations. Extra Hachis ( 最上級挽き肉 , Ekusutora Asshi , literally meaning "Highest-Rate Mincemeat") : Sanji performs a barrage of strong kicks from multiple directions at close range. This attack was first seen being used against Absalom . Jenga Ho ( ジェンガ砲 , Jenga Hō , literally meaning "Jenga Cannon") : Used as a follow-up to Zoro's Daibutsu Giri , Sanji kicks the pieces of the building at a giant enemy. This was first seen being used against Oars . This attack's name comes from the game Jenga , which the attack resembles. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Jenga Cannon . After the Timeskip This is a list showing the attacks and techniques Sanji used after the two years training in Kamabakka Kingdom . Jabot ( ヅャボ , Jyabo , literally meaning "Crop") : Sanji delivers a quick kick to the opponent's face. It was first used against Caribou when he tried to flirt with Nami . Hell Memories ( 地獄の思い出 , Heru Memorīzu , literally meaning "Memories of Hell") : After closing his eyes and recalling a painful memory, Sanji immediately becomes enraged, with his whole-body catching fire (resembling when he found out that Nami was kidnapped by Absalom ). He then leaps into the air and delivers a powerful kick to his opponent, setting their entire body on fire and causing significant damage. This attack generates an enormous amount of fire, even enough to encompass a gigantic opponent like Wadatsumi , whom it was first used to defeat. Unlike the non-canon Sexy Fire , it is unknown if the state Sanji enters when body catches fire is related to Diable Jambe, as he never mentions it. Sanji's first usage of this attack was triggered by him remembering his time on Momoiro Island , his second usage of it instead involved recalling his hatred for his family, and with his newfound genetic enhancements, was powerful enough to knock back Queen in his Ancient Zoan-enhanced form. Sky Walk ( 空中歩行 , Sukai Wōku , literally meaning "Sky Walk") : Sanji's version of Geppo . Sanji kicks the air to jump higher, giving the appearance of flight. Sanji created this technique during his ordeal in "hell" to escape from the okamas. It was first seen used to escape after being surrounded by the Sea Urchin-Armor Spine Squad, making them hurt themselves. Sanji's speed is boosted to extreme levels while performing this technique, as he was able to travel from downtown Dressrosa to the edge of Green Bit in less than a minute. In One Piece Film: Gold , Sanji states he cannot Sky Walk while carrying a heavy pack on his back, but later is shown carrying Nami and Carina with no difficulty (possibly due to excitement). Blue Walk ( 海歩行 , Burū Wōku , literally meaning "Sea Walk") : The underwater version of Sky Walk. Sanji kicks the water multiple times in order to achieve the effect of "running" underwater. This technique allows him to travel at incredible speeds, resulting in traveling at least as fast or faster than a fish-man underwater. According to Sanji, he created this technique after two years of constantly running away from okama . It was first seen used to reach the Kraken , just before using Diable Jambe - Bien Cuit: Grill Shot . It is shown that this technique can be used in Nami's body. Although this is the underwater version of the original Sky Walk, Sanji is seen using this move first in the series.

mastery through Enma's test, a Famous Blade notorious for draining the "Ryuo", their term for Armament Haki, from weaker swordsmen, turning them into a husk. It, in fact, drained the Haki from him, emaciating his right arm; however, Zoro retook all the drained Haki seconds later. During the Raid on Onigashima, he managed to gain some control over Enma, emanating a flame-like aura, and to wound Kaidou's dragon form, same
The user stares down a crowd and causes the weakest members to collapse.
The user’s spirit clashes with another and cracks the air between them.
his own ship a ways off, shocking Helmeppo, who did not notice it despite his own Observation Haki. On top of that, Koby also managed to sense Kyros' great strength despite just meeting him.

The Jara Jara no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to grow chains from their body, making them a Chain Human ( 鎖人間 , Kusari Ningen , Funimation: "Chain Guy") . It was eaten by Mad Treasure . Etymology Jarajara ( ジャラジャラ・じゃらじゃら ) is a Japanese onomatopoeia for the jangling of metal, such as chains. The Funimation adaptations name this fruit as the Chain-Chain Fruit . Strengths and Weaknesses The chains granted by the fruit can be used as powerful blunt weapons, should the user be able to wield them effectively. The chains can also be used as a sort of armor for self-defense. The user is also capable of tying things up with the chains, and tightening the chains telekinetically (the chains he does this with glow red). The ends of the chains can be made sharp, allowing the user to stab opponents and latch onto faraway surfaces. The user can make the chains various sizes from being the size of a average chains to being large enough to act like a bridge. Although the chains are strong, they can be broken with sufficient force. Otherwise, the user is affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Mad Treasure uses this fruit in combat, attacking people in a variety of ways. He also uses it to chain people up and capture them, and can crush them with the chains. His chains can also come in alternative forms, such as accessories like necklaces, sashes, and collars, which he use to make people do what he wants by tightening them. The chains can also have different kinds of ends, such as large claw-anchors to latch onto things or weights increase the power of their strikes. Mad Treasure can change the metal or material the chains are made of since he has been shown changing his into gold along with stating they can be changed into diamonds to further boost the chain's offensive and defensive capabilities. Techniques Mad Anchor ( マッド・アンカー , Maddo Ankā ) : Treasure shoots several chains at another ship and binds it to his, allowing him and his allies to cross the chains from their ship to the other. Chain Hammer ( チェーン・ハンマー , Chēn Hanmā ) : Treasure wraps chains around his arms and fists, giving his punches more power. Chain Lock ( チェーン・ロック , Chēn Rokku ) : Treasure wraps chains around a target, preventing them from moving. Mad Chain ( マッド・チェーン , Maddo Chēn ) : Treasure shoots a large amount of chains at his opponent so that they are struck by the tips of the chains. Gigantea ( ジガンティア , Jigantia ) : Treasure wraps chains around his entire body, giving him a durable armor, similar to a Mammillaria gigantea .

Three Sword Style Two Sword Style One Sword Style No Sword Style Nine Sword Style Team Combinations Anime Only Video Game Only These are techniques shown only in the anime version. List of Non-Canon Techniques No Sword Style Shinken Shirahatori ( 真剣白刃取り , Shinken Shirahatori , literally meaning "Taking a True Naked Sword") : A No Sword Style technique, Zoro catches the enemy blade with his bare hands. This was first seen being used twice against the Fanged Toad Pirates in an anime filler episode. However it failed both times, due to Sanji 's kick that made Zoro miss his timing. In the Funimation dub, it is called Sword Catch . Kokujo: O Tatsumaki ( 黒縄・大龍巻 , Kokujō: Ō Tatsu Maki , literally meaning "Kalasutra: Great Dragon Twister") : An arm-based version of Kokujo O Tatsu Maki and more powerful version of the No Sword Style Tatsu Maki empowered with Armament . Although it lacks the original version's cutting power, it can still send Zoro's enemies flying away with great force. It was first time used against Dice . This is called Black Rope Great Dragon Twister in the the Funimation subs, and Black Rope Great Tatsu Maki in the Funimation dub. Three Sword Style Gomu Gomu no Kaiten Ono ( ゴムゴムの回転斧 , Gomu Gomu no Kaiten Ono , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Rotating Axe") : An anime only combination of moves executed by Luffy , Zoro, Sanji, and Usopp . Luffy executes Gomu Gomu no Fūsen as well as twisting his body, using the stored air to launch himself into the air. Then, Usopp aboard the Going Merry fires a cannonball at Sanji, while Luffy shoots both of his arms towards the target. Sanji at this point Armeé de L'air Shoots the cannonball at Zoro who executes 108 Pound Ho to fire the cannonball up into the air and toward Luffy. Luffy catches the ball with his feet, the inertia of the cannonball spinning him vertically several times causing his legs to twist with each revolution. After all of the cannonball's inertia is gone, Luffy drops it and proceeds to spin forward releasing the full force of the stored energy from the winding, his body falling towards the target during all this. As Luffy approaches the target he stretches his legs out, which, through the speed of his spinning, creates the image of a large rotary blade. This attack impacts with the target, cutting it in half. The force of this seems out of Luffy's control. This was first used to destroy Ratchet 's castle. Hida Sanga Bakuretsu ( ヒダ山河獏列 , literally meaning "Secret Strike - Mountain River Tapir Line") : Used during the Pirate Baseball King featurette. Zoro holds three bats in his Three Sword Style and uses them to hit all three balls pitched by Hatchan at once, though he ends up cutting them instead. Dai Tetsugiri ( 大鉄斬り , Dai Tetsu-giri , literally meaning "Great Iron Cutter") : Zoro leaps backwards, performing four parallel vertical cuts below him. It was used to destroy Doran 's giant cannon on Spa Island . It is called Giant Iron Cut in the Funimation dub and Giant Buddha Slayer in the subs. Seiryu In: Ryusui ( 青龍印 流水 , Seiryū In: Ryūsui , literally meaning "Azure Dragon Seal: Running Water") : As a blue aura shaped like a dragon appears around him, Zoro charges towards his opponent and slashes them horizontally with his three swords, ending in a pose similar to Oni Giri. Seiryuu (read as "Qinglong" in pinyin) refers to the Azure Dragon , one of the four symbols of the Chinese constellations. It is also a wordplay on Seiryoin Ryusui . This was first seen being used to defeat Ain . However, during the attack, he was shown turning his sword so as to only hit Ain with the back of his blade, knocking her out instead of cutting her. It is called Azure Dragon Stamp Flowing Water in the Funimation dub, Blue Dragon Stamp Flowing Water in the subs, Mark of the Blue Dragon Running Water in the localized subs of One Piece: Pirate Warriors 3 , and Blue Dragon Seal Water Stream in the One Piece Card Game . One Sword Style Makie: Hana Arashi Sanjuroku Pound Ho ( 撒き餌 鼻嵐三十六 煩悩 鳳 , Makie: Hana Arashi Sanjūroku Pondo Hō , literally meaning "Bait: Nose Storm 36 Pound Cannon") : A combination technique between Zoro and Usopp and similar to the original 36 Pound Ho. Performed with Sogeking (Usopp) acting as one of Zoro's swords while holding one of his actual swords for him as he mounts and braces himself up one of Zoro's arms. With Sogeking stiffing up his body and acting as an extension of Zoro's blade, Zoro fires a narrower version of the normal 36 Pound Ho causing Sogeking discomfort afterward. It was first time used against Kaku and Jabra. When Zoro told Sogeking about this attack, Usopp's alter ego thought it was a really cool name, as makie ( 蒔絵 ) can also mean "Porcelain Decoration"; he never thought that in the end, Zoro was talking about the other meaning of makie ( 撒き餌 , "bait") , and the bait would be in fact the very Sogeking. In the Funimation dub, it is called Alure . Yoizamashi Katsu Batto ( 酔い醒まし喝破刀 , Yoizamashi Katsu Battō , literally meaning Sobering Up Expounding Sword) : Zoro swings his sword downward with great force, but just stops an inch from the opponent's face. The effect of the swing causes the opponent to be knocked out. It was first time used against Naomi Drunk . In the Funimation adaptations, it is called Sober-Up Stimulus Strike . Kokuto: Shi Shishi Sonson ( 黒刀・死・獅子歌歌 , Kokutō: Shi Shishi Sonson , literally meaning Black Blade: Death-Lion's Song) : Zoro turns his sword black by engulfing it with Armament and attacks with a Shi Shishi Sonson, a very powerful and swift unsheathing move. It was first time used to defeat Dice . Unlike the prior versions of Shishi Sonson, this version involves Zoro using this technique in normal grip instead of reverse grip, and also involves him wielding his sword with his right hand instead. Nine Sword Style Asura: Ugui ( 阿修羅穿威 , Ashura: Ugui , literally meaning "Asura: Drilling Might") : Zoro charges towards his opponent and slashes with all nine swords in a spin motion, like a drill, and ends in a stance similar to Makyūsen. This move was used against Indigo after Zoro blocked his Chemical Juggling techniques and had all 9 swords covered in said chemicals, similar to Yaki Oni Giri. Ugui ( 鯎 ) is a type of Japanese dace. It is called Ashura, Piercing Drill in the Funimation dub and Asura Piercing Drill in the English localization of One Piece: Pirate Warriors 3 . However, in that said game (as well as One Piece: Pirate Warriors 4 ) , it is only used by Zoro's timeskip version as his new inverted S2 (Trianglex2, Squarex3) finisher, despite it being used by his pre-timeskip-self in its first usage.

Haki Armament Haki Sabo has shown great mastery in the usage of Armament Haki, which he can imbue into his arm or his pipe. His mastery of this type of Haki is superior to that of Burgess, the captain of the Blackbeard Pirates' first ship, as he broke Burgess's Haki-infused right arm armor. Sabo also destroyed the Corrida Colosseum's fighting ring with his Haki-infused fists. Observation Haki Sabo possesses the
The user punches with metal knuckles to increase impact.
The user knocks out a charging animal with a single glare.
The user drives the shoulder into the opponent’s chest.
The user uses leverage to flip a larger opponent over their shoulder.

It is currently unknown if there is an actual way to awaken this Haki, since it cannot be learned, only controlled after one discovers that they are one in several million people to be born with it. So far, all seen cases of people awakening their Supreme King Haki involved them using the ability subconsciously while in a state of distress, but also with an unwavering will. has stated that although this Haki can be controlled, it cannot be trained to become stronger directly. Since it is, in essence, the embodiment of the user's spirit, it can only become stronger if the user themselves becomes stronger.
The user creates thin strings, controls targets, and slices objects from a distance.
The user becomes snow and covers the battlefield with freezing powder.

Haki As a Vice Admiral of the Marines, Garp is fully capable of using Haki. and is one of the very few people in the world who can use all three types of Haki. He is also a capable teacher, as he taught Kuzan, Koby and Helmeppo. Garp's Haki is potent enough to protect him from the deep-freezing effects of Kuzan's devil fruit abilities before easily breaking out of it.

ability_name: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix
ability_description: For other models of this fruit, see Tori Tori no Mi (Disambiguation) . The Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix is a Mythical Zoan -type Devil Fruit that grants the power to transform into a phoenix and half-phoenix hybrid at will, as well as self-regenerate (and heal others) using blue phoenix flames. The fruit was eaten by Marco . Etymology Tori ( 鳥 ) is Japanese for "bird". The kanji of the "Model" designation read fushichō ( 不死鳥 , lit. "immortal bird") , a Japanese title for the Western phoenix, and are glossed with the katakana for fenikkusu ( フェニックス ) , the common Japanese pronunciation of "phoenix" (borrowed from English, but based on the Latin pronunciation of the word). Appearance The Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix resembles a pineapple : an oval fruit with green skin segmented into round fruitlets, which each feature a signature Devil Fruit swirl in the middle and come to a prickly point on top. Instead of the T-shaped stem typically seen with Devil Fruits, this fruit possesses a crown of long, blade-shaped leaves that stiffly point up, form the occasional Devil Fruit swirl, and overall resemble the blue-yellow phoenix flames that this fruit grants access to. Transformations The ability user can transform into a mythical phoenix: a large, vaguely peafowl -shaped bird that is completely shrouded in blue (partially yellow) flames. This firebird form comes with two sizeable wings featuring eyespot feathers, a long and slender neck, a yellow-flamed head crest, legs with sharp talons, and a trio of long, yellow tail feathers made of closed, bound-together circles. Notably through Marco's use, no full-bodied Human-Beast Form has been seen (that evenly spreads over his entire body), only partial morphing of his arms and legs into wings and bird legs respectively; whether or not this partial transformation is in fact this Zoan's default Human-Beast Form remains unconfirmed. Beast Form. Human-Beast Form. Strengths As with all Zoans, the ability user's transformations greatly bolster their physical abilities, granting (in Marco's case) strength high enough to hold back two powerful Ancient Zoan users simultaneously. As a bird-type Zoan , it bestows the power of flight in both the Human-Beast and Beast Form, allowing the user to excel in matters of combat mobility and travel. Either one of the user's phoenix forms also comes with sharp sets of talons on the feet that can deliver slashing damage via kicks. The special "mythical" trait this Zoan confers upon the user is the ability to summon the blue flames of the phoenix—dubbed the "Blue Flames of Resurrection" ( 復活の青い炎 , Fukkatsu no Aoi Honō , VIZ: Blue Flame of Revival) and "Flames of Regeneration" ( 再生の炎 , Saisei no Honō , Funimation: Flames of Restoration) . The user is automatically clad in these flames upon transforming, but doing so is not strictly necessary for the flames to be conjured, at least in smaller amounts. Notably, while the phoenix flames do not burn, spread, or exude heat the way mundane fire does, their "special" nature makes them comparatively stronger; during a clash between Marco and Big Mom , Marco was able to blow away and cause pain to Prometheus , a living fire homie born from Big Mom's super-strong soul . Rather than burn, the phoenix flames have extraordinary healing power. When cloaked in them, the user can rapidly regenerate all injuries inflicted onto them—even mortal ones—much like a phoenix rising anew from its ashes. This makes the user enormously resilient and able to undergo extreme punishment without feeling pain or being slowed down. In Marco's case, devastating injuries like being riddled with bullets, having his head and torso run through with blades, chunks of his head and neck being blown off, or one of his wings being severed all have elicited little reaction from him, the flames erasing the damage about as quickly as it occurs. The ability user can enter their phoenix form to automatically heal all prior-accumulated damage in one go, or else heal by summoning the flames in isolation, without having to transform. In full phoenix mode, non- attacks may simply pass through the user's body as though it were pure flame, in a way superficially resembling a Logia 's invulnerability; the defining difference is that the user merely regenerates and retains a tangible, solid body beneath the fire. Thus, even injuries sustained under Seastone contact can be healed by the flames once contact with said material stops. In battle, the phoenix flames provide exceptional defense, their constant healing effect allowing the user to ward off extremely powerful attacks by using their own body—notably their large wings—as a shield. In doing so, Marco has successfully blocked and stood firm against overwhelming assaults like Marine Admiral Kizaru 's volley of light bullets , Admiral Akainu 's scorching magma fist , or the Emperor Kaidou 's castle-leveling breath of dragon fire . Moreover, while the blue flames lack the raw destructiveness of natural fire, they can still be wielded offensively, as Marco demonstrated by using fire-based attacks that, instead of burning, create powerful shockwaves and/or deliver blunt-force strikes that send opponents flying. Furthermore, any part of the phoenix body that is separated from the main body, like a wing, remains under the user's control, allowing it to be reshaped and used in a counterattack. Beyond self-recovery, the phoenix flames can be applied to treat others, even large groups of people if the user manually spreads out their fire in a large wave. When cast over someone else's injuries, the flames speed up the natural healing process to promote faster recovery, e.g. fixing a broken limb so that it is fully functional the next day. Once the flames have lit other people, they stay with and continuously heal them, the recipients' stamina permitting. This may even extend to diseases, as shown when Marco employed his fire as a temporary countermeasure against Queen the Plague 's cold-inducing virus , raising the body heat of all infected parties caught by his flames to halt the virus' spread and progression. That said, the ability user cannot mend others as well as they can their own body, the healing having a much smaller and slower effect on others. Weaknesses A downside of the phoenix flames is that, being purely for healing, they have none of the inherent attack potency (heat and ability to burn) of regular fire. However, they can still be used offensively to some degree and, owing to their "special" nature, can be used neutralize normal fire. While the user's regeneration can nullify most attacks, it has a limit. That is, the user is not truly immortal, suggesting that damage beyond a certain threshold (sustained all at once, over time, or both) might overwhelm the healing factor. Regardless, is established as a reliable counter to the regeneration: while attempts at healing or defense are still possible, the damage from -infused attacks persists. This was first demonstrated at Marineford where, despite ignoring hits from gunfire, a flying Marco was easily punched out of the air by Vice Admiral Garp , presumably using . During the Summit War and later the Raid on Onigashima , despite Marco instantly healing fatal wounds upon receiving them, he still accrued some minor damage over time, like scrapes and bruises, especially after facing more powerful foes; in the aftermath of either conflict, Marco was seen in bandages, hinting at injuries that he could or chose not to heal. Related to this, the regeneration may be slower if the user is caught off-guard or not transformed, as seen when Marco was pierced by Kizaru's lasers in his Human Form but did not instantly heal, despite the wounds' severity. Lastly, while the user can shrug off damage from attacks, they remain susceptible to restraint, e.g. being grabbed and held in place. The phoenix flames are known to draw upon the user's stamina, making prolonged use of them physically taxing until the flames can no longer be effectively created. On Onigashima , after fighting two powerful opponents for an extended period and having previously used his flames on dozens of other people, Marco was temporarily put out of commission, which onlookers attributed to his stamina being depleted. This also holds true for when the phoenix flames are used on others, being not as strong in their healing as when applied to the user's own body and fading once the recipient's stamina has run out. The flames' limited effect on others is also evident from how Marco was unable to help his elderly captain Whitebeard , who normally had to be hooked up to medical machinery as a result of his declining health. Otherwise, the user suffers from the standard weaknesses of any Devil Fruit user, like Seastone fully sealing their powers upon contact. Usage Marco's abilities are well-known and have garnered him the epithet Marco the Phoenix . He extensively relies on his transformation for aerial combat, and thanks to its physical and "mythical" qualities, his powers are highly versatile, allowing him to excel in offense, defense, and support roles during battle. Marco's bird physiology allow him to fly at high speeds and utilize his human legs to deliver strong -imbued kicks or knee strikes, and his phoenix legs to land slashing talon strikes. Marco can further employ his wings as a means of restraining foes, even extremely strong and fully transformed Ancient Zoan users, as seen when Marco easily subdued King and Queen —two of the strongest members of the Beasts Pirates with one billion-plus bounties —at once. For direct offense, Marco can shape his flames into shockwave-like blasts that, rather than burn opponents, deal concussive damage to or send them flying. In combat, Marco's regeneration serves a passive, defensive purpose, enabling him to outright ignore most physical attacks he is faced with. Ranged and melee blows, even weapons, pass right through him while any wounds received heal near-instantly, closing up without leaving a scar; this may make it seem even to formidable adversaries that Marco is invincible. Among other things, he has been observed shielding his allies using his own body and flames before simply healing himself up afterwards. In terms of healing others, Marco's flames have shown immense usefulness on battlefields with a lot of potential allies to be mended, whether from injury or infection. For years, Marco served as an efficient ship doctor for the Whitebeard Pirates , and following the crew's disbandment, he went on to do doctoring on Sphinx with his powers. Marco has made practical use of flying to traverse battlefields quickly and bypass enemies on the ground, in order to supervise whatever fighting is going on (as he did during the Summit War of Marineford in Whitebeard 's stead), to relay important intel, and to carry others with him. In his apprentice days, Marco acted as a scout for the Whitebeard Pirates, investigating unknown islands. Marco can seamlessly switch between his Beast or Human-Beast Forms. Unlike most Zoan users, in his half-phoenix form, he undergoes a more selective transformation, usually shifting just his arms into phoenix wings and his legs into phoenix legs, while his head and torso remain unchanged. If this is a mark of Marco's superior control over his powers or the Human-Beast Form's default look is unclear. Techniques Fujiazami ( 不 死 薊 , Fujiazami , literally meaning "Immortal Thistle") : A defensive move. Marco in phoenix mode jumps in front of someone he wishes to protect and, while midair, forms his wings into a shield-like vortex of flames to block an incoming attack. Being Marco's first-seen technique, Fujiazami was used by him (but not named) when protecting Whitebeard from Kizaru during the Summit War , warding off the admiral 's Yasakani no Magatama . It was named during the battle at Onigashima when Marco used the technique to intercept King 's Andon attack, shielding a bandaged-up Roronoa Zoro . Later on, the technique proved strong enough to block Kaidou 's Bolo Breath . The move's name is a pun on fujiazami ( 富士薊 ) , the name of the Japanese Fuji thistle . In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is named Undying Thistle . Hoo-in ( 鳳 凰 印 , Hōō-in , literally meaning "Fènghuáng Seal") : An aerial attack where Marco readies his phoenix talons in front of him in a seal-like pose, then delivers a devastating two-legged kick that causes a fiery shockwave. When first demonstrated, the move was strong enough to knock back the Queen Mama Chanter , a colossal ship (which in the anime involved Marco using a single leg and leaving a talon-like imprint on the ship). Later when used against Queen , Hoo-in was sent out from a distance as a fireball, striking the All-Star 's brachiosaurus neck with enough force to making him vomit blood. This attack's name contains the Japanese name for the fènghuáng ( hōō ), an immortal bird originating in Chinese mythology that, being often compared to the Western phoenix, is sometimes called "Chinese phoenix" (despite mythological similarities between the two being only superficial). In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this technique is called Phoenix Brand . Nashi no Tsubute ( 鳳梨 礫 , Nashi no Tsubute , literally meaning "Pineapple Stone") : A support technique where Marco whirls his phoenix wings around to disperse his flames over a wide area, allowing him to set alight and heal multiple people. He used this to counteract Queen 's Ice Oni virus in those infected on the Skull Dome's Live Floor , keeping the flames at just the right temperature to slow the virus' effects. However, the flames draw on the stamina of recipients, ceasing to work once it is depleted. The technique's name is a twofold pun: the kanji include hōri ( 鳳梨 ) , an antiquated Japanese word for pineapple, which plays on Marco's supposed likeness to said fruit and itself contains the kanji hō ( 鳳 ) for "fèng" (a fènghuáng's male aspect); however, the furigana indicate the reading Nashi no Tsubute ( 梨の礫 ) , which is an idiom that literally means "tossing the pear" and refers to a lack of reply after a question (the "nashi" for "pear" sounds the same as "nashi" for "nothing"). In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Phoenix Pyreapple . Bluebird ( 青炎雁 , Burūbādo , literally meaning "Blue-Flamed Goose") : An attack where Marco, using an outstretched index and middle finger, remotely shapes his phoenix flames into a dense, swirling mass in front of him, then launches it straight towards his enemy in a thrust-like, concussive strike. When performing Bluebird against King, Marco used one of his cut-off wings as a medium for the fireball, producing it from the wing's feathers and slamming it into King with immense force, sending the All-Star flying. In a later (anime-only) use of the attack against Queen, the flames morphed into a firebird, which flapped its wings as it flew toward the All-Star but was easily sliced apart by Queen with his sword. The bluebird is a passerine bird named for its blue plumage. The attack's name may also reference (given the kanji for "goose") how in Chinese myth, especially per ancient texts, the fènghuáng is said to bear various animal resemblances, e.g. having a goose's breast. Ongle ( 鶴爪 , Onguru , literally meaning "Crane Claw") : A melee attack similar to Hoo-in . Marco performs a flying, single-legged kick at an opponent while his talons do slashing damage. This kick proved strong enough to blow the All-Star Queen away with great force, while gashing his face. Ongle is French for "nail". The attack's name (the kanji) may play on how the fènghuáng is said, per modern descriptions, to be the composite of various birds, including having a crane's legs. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Crane Talons . Non-Canon Techniques Power Leg Claw ( パワーレッグクロー , Pawā Reggu Kurō ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco kicks his opponent with his talons while in his Human-Beast Form. Power Leg Spin ( パワーレッグスピン , Pawā Reggu Supin ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco performs a roundhouse kick with his talons while in his Human-Beast Form. Power Leg Shoot ( パワーレッグシュート , Pawā Reggu Shūto ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco delivers a sweeping kick with his talons while in his Human-Beast Form. Phoenix Spear ( フェニックススピア , Fenikkusu Supia ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco flies forward in his Human-Beast Form to deliver a flying kick to his opponent. Dive Attack ( ダイブアタック , Daibu Atakku ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco while in his Human-Beast Form divebombs into his opponent from above, then shifts into Human Form to repeatedly strike them with his bare hands. Air Raid Smash ( エアーレイドスマッシュ , Eā Reido Sumasshu ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , where Marco turns into his Human-Beast Form, spins around to draw his opponent in, kicks them into the air, flies up to them, and then kicks them back down to the ground. Saisei no Hono ( 再生の炎 , Saisei no Honō , literally meaning "Flames of Regeneration") : Used as his Level 1 Special Attack in Pirate Warriors 2 and 3 , where Marco changes into a phoenix and flaps his wings, restoring some of his health while sending out a shockwave. The technique reappears in Pirate Warriors 4 under the same name. In the games' English releases, the technique is called Rebirth Flame (in 2 and 3 ) and Flame of Restoration (in 4 ). Tensei no Soen ( 転生の蒼炎 , Tensei no Sōen , literally meaning "Blue Flame of Transmigration") : Marco changes into a phoenix, spirals around enemies to gather them into the air, then charges into the ground from a very high altitude, unleashing a shockwave. It is used in Pirate Warriors 2 and 3 as his Level 2 Special Attack, it reappears as a Special Attack for Marco in Pirate Warriors 4 . In the games' English releases, the technique is called Blue Transmigration Flame (in 2 and 3 ) and Transmigration Flame (in 4 ). Fushicho no Goen ( 不死鳥の豪炎 , Fushichō no Gōen , literally meaning "Phoenix's Great Flame") : While airborne in his Human-Beast Form, Marco shoots a fiery shockwave forward from his wings, suffering some slight recoil in the process. The move is seen in Pirate Warriors 3 as Marco's Kizuna Rush Special Attack and likewise reappears in Pirate Warriors 4 , as part of Marco's Special Attack set. In the game's English releases, the move is called Phoenix Inferno ( 3 ) and Phoenix Ignition ( 4 ). Fushicho no Mezame ( 不死鳥の目覚め , Fushichō no Mezame , literally meaning "Phoenix's Awakening") : Featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , this is Marco's Serious Burst mode, which causes him to remain floating in the air in his Human-Beast Form (and is activated while midair). In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Awakening . Fushicho no Yokugeki ( 不死鳥の翼撃 , Fushichō no Mezame , literally meaning "Phoenix's Wing Attack") : A Special Attack featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , Marco in his Human-Beast Form flies circles across the ground, sweeping enemies away with a vortex of flame. In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Raid . Fushicho no Shugeki ( 不死鳥の蹴撃 , Fushichō no Shūgeki , literally meaning "Phoenix's Kicking Attack") : A Special Attack featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , Marco doles out a barrage of consecutive kicks while in his Human-Beast Form, sending enemies in front of him flying back. In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Kick . Fushicho no Kyoshu ( 不死鳥の強襲 , Fushichō no Kyōshū , literally meaning "Phoenix's Assault") : A Special Attack featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , Marco changes into a full phoenix and charges forward while spinning about his own axis, knocking enemies back. In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Assault . Zoan-kei Genjushu ( 「 動物 系」幻獣種 , Zōn-kei Genjūshu , literally meaning "Animal System Mythical Beast Species") : Marco soars into the air, knocking the enemy upwards, then moves to the airborne enemy to kick them down to the ground like he did with Kizaru during the Summit War of Marineford . This attack appears in Burning Blood and in the English version is called Mythical Zoan . Genju Rengeki ( 幻獣連撃 , Genjū Rengeki , literally meaning "Mythical Beast Chain Attack") : Marco flies towards an enemy to dole out rapid consecutive hits in the process of colliding with the enemy. This move appears in Burning Blood and, in the game's English release, is called Beast Chain . The following were techniques of Marco uniquely featured in the discontinued Japanese-only One Piece Thousand Storm mobile game: The following EX-ranked Special Moves: Soen no Kenjo ( 蒼炎の拳浄 , Sōen no Kenjō , lit. "Blue Flame's Purging Fist") Soen no Tenkei ( 蒼炎の天恵 , Sōen no Tenkei , lit. "Blue Flame's Blessing") Soen no Kago ( 蒼炎の加護 , Sōen no Kago , lit. "Blue Flame's Divine Protection") Genjushu no Hidariashi ( 幻獣種の左脚 , Genjūshu no Hidariashi , lit. "Mythical Beast's Left Leg") Soen no Dageki ( 蒼炎の墜撃 , Sōen no Dageki , lit. "Blue Flame's Crashing Strike") Fushicho Ranbu ( 不死鳥乱舞 , Fushichō Ranbu , lit. "Phoenix's Wild Dance") Tensei no Hono ( 転生の炎 , Tensei no Honō , lit. "Flame of Rebirth") as an unraked Special Move The following were unlockable as basic non-Special Moves: Fushicho no Shukuen ( 不死鳥の祝炎 , Fushichō no Shukuen , lit. "Phoenix's Blessing Flame") in both EX-ranked and EX+-ranked forms Fushicho no Shumai ( 不死鳥の襲舞 , Fushichō no Shūmai , lit. "Phoenix's Dancing Assault") Soen Soso ( 蒼炎双爪 , Sōen Sōsō , lit. "Blue-Flamed Twin Claws") Fushicho Kassho ( 不死鳥滑翔 , Fushichō Kasshō , lit. "Phoenix's Soar") Soen no Uzu ( 蒼炎の渦 , Sōen no Uzu , lit. "Blue Flame Vortex") Fushicho no Tsume ( 不死鳥の爪 , Fushichō no Tsume , lit. "Phoenix Talon") Chugi no Tate ( 忠義の盾 , Chūgi no Tate , lit. "Shield of Loyalty") History Three decades ago, a 15-year-old Marco had already eaten the fruit and seemed well-versed in utilizing his phoenix transformation. Using its power, he became reputed as Marco the Phoenix.
The user spins and delivers a heavy kick to the opponent’s ribs.
The user freezes seawater and creates ice weapons from the air.
The user’s willpower-charged attack collides before physical contact happens.

ability_name: Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu
ability_description: The Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu is a Mythical Zoan -type Devil Fruit that allows its user to transform into a hybrid and full Azure Dragon at will. Belonging to the "strongest class of Devil Fruit", it was once a World Noble treasure before it was stolen by Charlotte Linlin and eaten by Kaidou during the God Valley Incident 38 years ago. At some point, Dr. Vegapunk extracted a sample from a captive Kaidou's Lineage Factor and, with it, successfully created an artificial replica of this Devil Fruit . However, the given transformation was not of the same color as the original, and so solely for this reason the result was deemed a failure by Vegapunk, accidentally tricking the World Government into thinking the fruit was powerless. The replica was eventually consumed by Kouzuki Momonosuke , causing him to obtain a dragon transformation nigh-identical to that of Kaidou, save for its color.
