Hovering the symbol gives further details. This type of Haki grants the user the ability to dominate the wills of others. Unlike the other types of Haki, Supreme King Haki cannot be attained through training, instead its users must be born with the ability and can only learn to control it after discovering that they have it. Only one in several million people are born with this Haki.
The user waits until hostile intent spikes and then counters immediately.
The user bends a segmented blade around shields.
The user becomes a spider-like creature and traps enemies with webs.

Hasshoken is a martial art used by the Chinjao Family . It is a martial art that allows the user to produce powerful shockwaves. Users Description According to Gatz , this fighting style allows the user to control the vibrations of the whole body, allowing them to create powerful shock waves. These shock waves are powerful enough to destroy a metal shield or easily defeat a strong man. Sai also claimed that these attacks are unblockable and reverberating, causing defenses to shatter. The only drawback is that the shockwaves can be countered by a significant amount of force, as Sai's shockwaves were countered by Ideo's explosive punches, and Chinjao's every shockwave was countered during his fight with Luffy in Block C. Variations This fighting style can be used on any part of the user's body, be it the head, fists, legs or elbows. Or on the whole body at once. Sai using the Hasshoken through his kicks. Boo using his Hasshoken through his elbow. Techniques Chinjao's Techniques As the 12th head of the Happo Navy, Chinjao has complete mastery over the Hasshoken techniques. His usage of the style is focused on his head, particularly taking advantage of the drill-shape. Buto ( 武頭 , Butō , literally meaning "Martial Head") : Chinjao jumps in reverse and headbutts the enemy. The strike is strong enough to stop Cavendish 's Durandal and creates a shockwave while clashing. It was first seen used against Cavendish in Corrida Colosseum. It is unknown if he uses vibrations in this attack. This is called Head-Arms in the Viz manga and FUNimation adaptations. For the chapter titled "Open Chinjao", see Chapter 719 . Hasshoken Ogi: Kiryu Kirikugi ( 八衝拳奥義 錐龍錐釘 , Hasshō-ken Ōgi: Kiryū Kirikugi , literally meaning "Eight Impacts Fist Secret Technique: Drill Dragon Drill Nail") : A secret Hasshoken technique that Chinjao was able to perform thanks to his drill-shaped head. Chinjao hardens his head with Armament and headbutts the target. It was strong enough to split open layers of ice that not even fire or axes could breach, and was the "key", his Open Chinjao ( 開けチンジャオ , Hirake Chinjao ) , to Chinjao's treasure. After his head was flattened by Garp during their duel thirty years ago, he lost the ability to perform the attack. After his head was reshaped by Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Thor Elephant Gun , he regained the ability to use this technique, and was shown using it in combination with Elizabello II 's Light Version King Punch to shatter Pica 's stone statue arm . However, he lost this ability again when he tried to attack Baby 5 and Sai broke his head to protect her. It is unnecessary to open Jewel Sheet Ice with this technique anymore because this technique passed to Sai. This is called Hasshoken Secret Art: Drill Dragon Nail in the Viz manga and the FUNimation adaptations. Hasshoken Ogi: Mukiryu Mukirikugi ( 八衝拳奥義 無錐龍無錐釘 , Hasshō-ken Ōgi: Mukiryū Mukirikugi , literally meaning "Eight Impacts Fist Secret Technique: Drill-less Dragon Drill-less Nail") : After Chinjao's head was flattened and he realized he could no longer open up the ice above his treasure, Chinjao renamed his technique. It was seen in this form during his fight with Luffy in the Corrida Colosseum , with the intent to kill the young pirate. Like the original technique, Chinjao hardens his head with Armament and headbutts the target, but the power is greatly diminished. Although it lacked the characteristic concentric circle vibration lines of other Hasshoken techniques, Chinjao still referred to it as a Hasshoken technique. It became obsolete once Chinjao's head was restored to its drill-shape. This is called Non-Drill Dragon Non Nail in the Viz manga and FUNimation subs. In the FUNimation dub of Episode of Sabo , this is called Hasshoken Secret Art: Non-Drill Dragon Non Nail . In the FUNimation dub of the main series, this is called Hasshoken Technique: Non-Drill Dragon Non Nail . Anime-Only Techniques Buto Kaiten ( 武頭回転 , Butō Kaiten , literally meaning "Martial Head Spin) : Chinjao plants his head into the ground and does a head stand, put his hands together and legs apart, and spins like a top. He then moves towards his opponent like a tornado. The force of the attack sent all of the unconscious gladiators around him flying. This is called Head-Arms Spin in the FUNimation adaptations. Buto Kaiten: Ranbu ( 武頭回転乱舞 , Butō Kaiten: Ranbu , literally meaning "Martial Head Spin: Wild Dance") : A variant of Buto Kaiten where Chinjao spins faster and has more control over his movements, allowing him to turn more quickly and spin towards his opponent at great speed, occasionally jumping through the air. Luffy stopped the spinning by wrapping his arm around Chinjao's entire body. This is called Head-Arms Spin: Wild Dance in the FUNimation adaptations. Sai's Techniques As the 13th head of the Happo Navy, Sai has been trained extensively in the Hasshoken style. Upon successfully bending his grandfather and predecessor's legendary drill-shaped head with his own technique, Chinjao comes to recognize Sai has awakened his true powers and bestows him the true secrets of the Hasshoken technique. His usage of the style is focused on his leg, which has inherited the ultimate technique. Bujaogen ( 武脚跟 , Bujaogen , literally meaning "Martial Leg Heel") : Sai kicks his target while emitting the Hasshoken shock wave. This allows him to shatter defense while kicking through his target. It was first used against an unnamed gladiator during Block C's match, but was not named until he used it (along with Armament ) to block his grandfather's Kiryu Kirikugi , which was able to bend even Chinjao's legendary drill-shaped head. This is called Wujiaogen Heel in the Viz manga and FUNimation adaptations. Hasshoken Ogi: Kiryu Kirikugi ( 八衝拳奥義 錐龍錐釘 , Hasshō-ken Ōgi: Kiryū Kirikugi , literally meaning "Eight Impacts Fist Secret Technique: Drill Dragon Drill Nail") : The Hasshoken secret technique bestowed upon his leg upon proving his might against his predecessor. Sai performs cartwheels while imbuing his leg with Armament , and then performs a kick with vibrations emitting from said leg. Chinjao claimed that with this, Sai should be able to break apart the layers of ice in the same way Chinjao did 30 years back. This technique was first used when Chinjao bestowed him the Hasshoken's secret technique upon his leg, defeating Lao G and sending a massive shock wave linear to the kick, as well as creating a fissure along the ground. Sai using Bujaogen to intercept Chinjao. Sai using Hasshoken Ogi: Kiryu Kirikugi to defeat Lao G.
The user coats their fist in invisible spiritual armor before striking.

The Pamu Pamu no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to cause their own body or any inorganic object they touch to rupture and explode , turning them into a Puncture Human ( 破裂 人間 , Panku Ningen , Viz: "Rupture-Man"; Funimation: "Rupture Man") . It was eaten by Gladius . Etymology Pamu Pamu comes from Pum or Pan Pan , a Japanese onomatopoeia for bursting. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, it is called the Pop-Pop Fruit , a reference on how this power causes targets to pop. Strength and Weaknesses The main strength of this power is that it allows the user to cause anything inorganic they touch to swell up, which will then rupture and explode violently. The user can also apply it to themselves. The user remains unharmed from the explosion. The user can rupture themselves but cannot rupture any organic material and living materials, unless they have been converted into toys , which turns their bodies into inorganic material. Also, any swellings created by this technique will detonate only as long as the user remains conscious; should the user lose consciousness before the swelling ruptures, it would go back down to normal levels. Finally, the inflating and rupturing can be stopped if the user lets go of the target, allowing the object to deflate itself. Other than that, this fruit possesses the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Gladius uses this ability for combat. He can cause his own arms to swell up and explode while grabbing onto his opponents, causing explosive damage, or grab onto an object and cause it to work as a grenade by launching shrapnel with the explosion injuring his opponents. If a person is changed into a toy then it is considered an inorganic object, enabling Gladius to make them swell up and explode with his ability, thus killing them. Techniques Met Punc ( メットパンク , Metto Panku ) : Gladius swells up his helmet and causes it to explode which causes the shards from the explosion to hit the target. This was first seen used against Monkey D. Luffy and Viola when they were running away from Gladius. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Helmet Pop . Jirai Punc ( 地雷パンク , Jirai Panku , literally meaning "Landmine Punc") : Gladius implants his powers within a surface, creating swelling points. These swelling points will explode if they are touched. This was first used to arm walls of Dressrosa palace's plateau, to which Chinjao fell victim to when he attempted to climb it. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Mine Pop . Punc Bala ( 破裂弾丸 , Panku Bāra , literally meaning "Rupture Bullet") : Gladius implants his powers on bullets, causing them to swell up and float, similar to balloons. Those inflated bullets will then detonate on contact. Gladius can also use this technique in order to gain altitude, by jumping from bullet to bullet. It was first used against Nico Robin , Bartolomeo , and Rebecca when they were floating above the 3rd level of the new royal plateau. Bala is Spanish, Catalan, and Filipino for "bullet". In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Pop Baller . Punc Rock Fest ( パンク 岩 フェス , Panku Rokku Fesu ) : Gladius inflates the ground around him and then blows it up, causing massive destruction to his surroundings. First used against Cavendish , who managed to escape the attack thanks to Bartolomeo's ability . In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Pop Rock Fest . Catapult Punc ( 投石 パンク , Kataparuto Panku , literally meaning "Stone Throwing Punc") : Using the firing mechanism installed on his arms, Gladius fires several inflating bullets at the enemy. First used against Monkey D. Luffy, but Bartolomeo jumped in front of the bullets and took the damage instead. It was later named after he used it against Nico Robin, but Bartolomeo was able to stop it with his barrier. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Catapult Pop . Bracchium ( ブラッキウム , Burakkiumu ) : Gladius inflates his wrist devices to swell up and explode. It was first seen against the dwarves of Tontatta but named against Cavendish, but Bartolomeo intervened. Punc Hair ( パンクヘア , Panku Hea ) : Gladius inflates his hair, transforming each strand into sharp needles, which he then fires at his opponents, made more dangerous by the paralyzing agent they are coated in. First used against Bartolomeo, who tried to stop Gladius from destroying the rock wall Robin and Cavendish were on. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Pop Hair . Fashion Punc ( 全身破裂 , Fasshon Panku , literally meaning "Fullbody Rupture") : Gladius swells up himself, turning himself into a gigantic balloon-like appearance. If he is attacked in any way while in this form, his body will cause a massive explosion, which will also fire off all of his hair needles in all directions. The aftereffect is that all of his hair will be gone, leaving behind a buzz cut. This was first seen in an attempt to attack Bartolomeo and Robin at the same time. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Fashion Pop . Punc Rock Super Arena ( パンク 岩 スーパーアリーナ , Panku Rokku Sūpā Arīna ) : A stronger version of Punc Rock Fest , Gladius causes an area to swell up to great proportions before detonating it. This was first seen in an attempt to attack Robin, but she evaded it thanks to Hakuba 's speed. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Pop Rock Super Arena .
The user releases chilling energy that freezes opponents on contact.

The Kama Kama no Mi is an anime-only Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that gives the user long, sharp nails that they can use to create slicing air blades. It was eaten by Eric . Etymology "Kama" ( 鎌 ) means "sickle" in Japanese. In the context of this power, "kama" is derived from the word kamaitachi ( 鎌鼬 , lit. "sickle weasel") , a Japanese yōkai thought to come and go with/ride on the wind and use its razor-sharp, sickle-like claws to slash at human victims. The word has entered Japanese parlance as a general term referring to cuts in the skin inflicted by whirlwinds. An alternate term is kamakaze ( 鎌風 , lit. "sickle wind") , which has also been used in explaining the Rankyaku technique. The fruit is called Sickle-Sickle Fruit in the Funimation adaptations and Slash-Slash Fruit in the Odex dub. Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this fruit is that it (permanently) lengthens and sharpens the user's nails, which are capable of creating razor-sharp gusts capable of cleaving through even hard materials like bedrock. According to Eric, his wind blades are as sharp as a Famous Blade . The claw-like nails themselves can also be used to slice opponents in close-quarters combat, as seen when Eric cut a retreating Marine. This fruit does not seem to have any specific weaknesses aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses, although Eric is seen filing his nails in concept art, hinting that he has to keep them sharp in order to use his powers. Usage This Devil Fruit gives Eric sharp nails to cut his enemies, both directly or by slashing through the air to create sharp air blades. Despite the cutting power it offers, Luffy was able to withstand Eric's attacks with ease. Techniques Kama Kama no Tsumujikaze ( カマカマの旋風 , Kama Kama no Tsumijikaze , literally meaning "Sickle Sickle Whirlwind") : Eric releases a powerful air blade that slashes the enemy multiple times upon contact. It is strong enough to slice through rock. He can also launch a variation with two of them in a "X" formation. This is called Slash-Slash Whirlwind in the Odex dub and Sickle-Sickle Whirlwind in the Funimation adaptations. Kama Kama no Kamaitachi ( カマカマの鎌鼬 , Kama Kama no Kamaitachi , literally meaning "Sickle Sickle Wind Scythe") : Eric releases a single small air blade that nonetheless is still powerful enough to slice through rock. In the Funimation adaptations, this is called Sickle-Sickle Wind Slash . Kama Kama no Kamaitachi Midareuchi ( カマカマの鎌鼬乱れ撃ち , Kama Kama no Kamaitachi Midaeruchi , literally meaning "Sickle Sickle Wind Scythe Chaotic Strike") : Eric's ultimate technique and a stronger version of Kama Kama no Kamaitachi . He slashes many times, releasing multiple air blades toward the enemy before unleashing a powerful whirlwind of razor air that can effortlessly slice through solid rock. This is called Slash Slash Weasel's Slash in the Odex dub and Sickle-Sickle Wind Slash: Rapid Fire in the Funimation adaptations.
The user uses a reverse sword grip for close-range cuts.

The Zuma Zuma no Mi is a non-canon Devil Fruit of unknown type, allowing the user to control plasma, making the user a Plasma Human ( プラズマ人間 , Purazuma Ningen ) . It was eaten by Smash . Etymology "Zuma" comes from the Japanese way of pronouncing the word "plasma" ( プラズマ , purazuma ) . Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this Devil Fruit is that it allows the user to generate, manipulate, and transform their body into plasma. Aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses, there seem to be no known weaknesses to this power. Usage The only known use by Smash is to create large, electricity-emitting balls of plasma.

Man-Demon Tactics is Gin 's fighting style. The style centers around his tonfa and incorporates his dexterity and strength. Overview Gin's tonfas act like hammers, gaining more power from Gin spinning them by their handles and building up momentum for his swing. Combined with his own abilities, Gin is a very deadly opponent when using these, as shown when he smashed Pearl 's iron shields with a single blow while moving so quickly that he took Pearl by surprise. This is a testament to his strength, as even Sanji could not make a scratch on Pearl's shields. Techniques Kijin Ranbu ( 鬼人乱舞 , Kijin Ranbu , literally meaning "Demonic Dance") : Gin leaps forward at his enemy with one of his tonfas swinging and lands in a crouched position, slamming one of them down onto the opponent or ground in front of him. This technique was first used against Sanji, but it was only given a non-canon name in One Piece Treasure Cruise . Video Game Only Krieg Kantai Delta Explosion ( クリーク艦隊・デルタエクスプロージョン , Kurīku Kantai Deruta Ekusupurōjon , literally meaning "Krieg Fleet Delta Explosion") : Working in tandem with Krieg and Pearl , the three charge an opponent simultaneously. Gin strikes with his tonfa, Krieg with his Daisenso, and Pearl with one of his handheld shields. This attack can focus on a single enemy or damage multiple enemies at once. The technique appears in One Piece Treasure Cruise .
The user transforms into a mammoth and crushes enemies with tusks.

Notably, Haki originates from an individual's spirit and not their physical body, so even if a person's mind is transferred into another body, they would still be capable of using their Haki to the same degree that they normally can, even if the body they transferred into is not a Haki user. Haki is separated into three types, or "colors" , which gives the user a sixth sense of the world around them, allowing them to sense the presence, strength, and emotions of other people, even if they are out of sight or far away. It also grants limited precognitive abilities, allowing the user to sense their opponents' intentions and predict their actions and attacks before they happen.

For other uses, see Zou (Disambiguation) . The Zou Zou no Mi is a Zoan -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to transform into an elephant hybrid and a full elephant at will. It was "eaten" by Spandam 's sword, Funkfreed , through the process developed by Vegapunk . Etymology "Zo" ( 象 , Zō ) means "elephant" in Japanese. In the Viz Manga and FUNimation adaptations, it is called the Elephant-Elephant Fruit . However in Volume 81 of the Viz Manga, it is renamed to Eleph-Eleph Fruit . Strengths and Weaknesses The most basic strength of the fruit is that it gives its user the abilities of an elephant, including an increase in size and raw strength. In the case of Spandam 's sword, the fruit's most major strength is that it has given life to an otherwise inanimate object. This means he can call Funkfreed whenever he wants even if the sword is not close to him. With this, Spandam has been given a unique weapon with which to fight. The fruit so far does not seem to have any specific weaknesses outside the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. The animal created from this fruit, however, has somewhat of a minor weakness with it though, considering the circumstances around it. While the fruit has given Spandam's sword the strength and intelligence of an elephant, the fruit inadvertently also in a sense gave the sword some common sense in the matters of fearing physical pain and death. This was best seen when Franky threatened to shoot Funkfreed in the face. While the sword may not have been alive to begin with, it was afraid of being killed by Franky like a real elephant would be in such a situation. In other words, the fear of being killed was instilled into the sword thanks to the power of the fruit. Usage The fruit's abilities have primarily been used for combat and as Funkfreed is a sword, they are also used second-hand by his master Spandam in a style of swordsmanship known as Funkfreed Fencing ( 象剣 剣術 , Fankufurīdo Kenjutsu , literally meaning "Elephant Sword Fencing") . Techniques Ivory Dart ( 象牙突進 , Aiborī Dāto , literally "Ivory Rush") : A non-lethal move intended to disable Nico Robin . Funkfreed extends his body rapidly (stretching in a manner similar to a Gomu Gomu no Mi user) to deliver a powerful headbutt, catching Robin between his trunk and tusks. Another possibility of this move is if his trunk is a sword, then the move could trap and slice the target. The kanji's original reading is "Zouge Tosshin", with the kanji for the former two literally meaning "elephant fang/tusk". Elephant Chop ( エレファント・チョップ , Erafanto Choppu ) : Spandam swings the sword over his head, and Funkfreed morphs into his elephant-sword hybrid form, and rushes forward to spear an opponent with his sword-trunk. This move is strong enough to wound Franky, who is immune to bullets, while he is blocking. Nevertheless, Franky still showed it was stoppable. Anime and Manga Differences In the anime, the elephant form that Funkfreed transforms into using the Devil Fruit's powers is colored white. However, on the cover of volume 42 and in Episode of Merry , it is shown that the elephant form is naturally gray colored. This difference in color between manga and anime is partly due to the common fact that the intended colors came out much later after the anime. This is also due to the fact that Funkfreed's elephant form resembles greatly a stereotypical white elephant, commonly revered in India, in the uncolored pages of the manga.

Overview Techniques Gear 2 Gear 3 Gear 4 Gear 5 Team Combinations Gear 5 ("Gear Fifth") is the awakened form of the Hito Hito no Mi, Model: Nika , which strengthens the user's rubbery body. It also allows the user the ability to manipulate and transform their environment, similar to a Paramecia 's awakening. Luffy achieved this ability during his final battle with Kaidou on Onigashima . Overview The awakening of the Gomu Gomu no Mi , formerly known as the Hito Hito no Mi, Model: Nika , grants the user's rubber body increased strength and freedom, allowing them to fight in any way they please. It is for this reason that the user is said to become the " Warrior of Liberation " ( 解放の戦士 , Kaihō no Senshi ) , bringing joy and freedom to those around them, and this fruit's power being described as " the most ridiculous power in the world " ( 世界で最もふざけた能力 , sekai de mottomo fuzaketa nōryoku ) . In this state, Luffy's heartbeat takes on a musical rhythm, doddottotto ( ドッドットット ) , which has been described by Zunesha as the " Drums of Liberation " ( 解放のドラム , Kaihō no Doramu ) . His hair, shirt, and shorts turn white, his eyes gain red pupils (pink in the anime) that are occasionally depicted as glowing bright red, and white clouds float around his neck and under his arms, similar to the clouds of steam that accompany him in Gear 4. In the anime, his belt turns purple if it is not already. His eyebrows also become swirled and billowing. It seems Gear 5 sometimes transforms Luffy's outfit to his pants and sandals that he usually wears as opposed to whatever else he is wearing, which is not unlike the article of clothing removal for most Zoan transformations. The outfit will return upon deactivating Gear 5. To activate this transformation, Luffy forces his heart to beat to the rhythm of the Drums of Liberation. When Luffy either activates this state or is in it for long periods of time, he exudes joyous and contagious jubilation and mirth, He tends to laugh much more and feels light and extremely creative. Abilities The powers of the awakened Hito Hito no Mi, Model: Nika appear to be centered around freedom; Luffy himself has described his Gear 5 transformation as where he is completely free. Jewelry Bonney 's imitation of Gear 5 is even based on her ability to imagine herself with the most freedom. Most notably, its power allowed Luffy to overwhelm the former Emperor Kaidou , who held the title of " World's Strongest Creature " and whose strength was further enhanced by the Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu , in battle, and fight effectively against an admiral like Kizaru , who has the power of the Pika Pika no Mi . The fruit's true power, however, does not lie in the rubbery physiology it grants. Rather, it allows the user to turn whatever they can imagine into reality. Notably, the user's capabilities in combat are only limited by their imagination, allowing them to fight however they want in ways that defy common sense. As an awakened Mythical Zoan based on a God, Luffy is afforded immense strength, speed, and durability in Gear 5 that outclass the physical enhancement granted by most Zoan powers, including awakened ones, as well as his own Gear 4 . By comparison, his strength and speed is such that he can trade blows with and completely overpower Rob Lucci 's own awakened Zoan form with immense ease. In terms of durability, he could as well take a direct hit from Kaidou's Bolo Breath , only to come out of it virtually unharmed (despite his body appearing cartoonishly charred), and ingest a laser while only suffering brief discomfort from its heat. The awakening of the fruit also grants a faster recovery rate, which functions involuntarily, even when Luffy is unconscious. In fact, the physical feats Luffy can pull off in Gear 5 border on nearly the absurd; he can spin his legs fast enough to run through midair while generating enough friction to ignite fire in his wake, and spin like a top with such force that he turns into a burrowing drill. During the Egghead Arc , Luffy was even able to float in mid-air. However, to move, besides some limited short-range mid-air dodges, he seems to actively need to propel himself for long-range movement, like the aforementioned mid-air run, or through his classic method of grabbing onto a distant target and pulling himself forward. Even more ridiculously, like how Gear 5 lets Luffy pull off feats in a cartoonish manner, his body is affected in all manner of funky ways when reacting to physical and emotional stimulations. For example, when Luffy got hit by Topman Warcury 's Supreme King -imbued roar, most of his physical features, such as his clothes, eyes, cloud collar, and scars, were literally blown off of him before he physically reattached them. Outside of his rubbery attributes, Luffy can mitigate damage in cartoonish ways as well, like when he literally ate one of Kizaru's lasers and dispersed it as a flash of light throughout his body. Aligning with the Mythical Zoan nature of this form, Luffy's already rubbery body gains freedom and flexibility that surpasses what he would receive from his previous forms, to the point that it is now even cartoonishly malleable, with Kaidou likening it to " something out of a picture book ". Examples of this are when he took a hit to the head from Kaidou's Hassaikai , only for his head to warp and wrap around the weapon and its spikes, and when Kaidou tried to smash him into the ground, his body was simply squished instead and later returned to its original shape. This mitigates a portion of the damage dealt by attacks that would normally bypass the defensive abilities of his Devil Fruit, though the impacts will still hurt and can cause damage. When using Gear 5, Luffy can utilize both the limb inflation of Gear 3 and the muscle inflation of Gear 4 (called Muscle-Muscle Power in the anime ) without the need to pump air into his body first, allowing him to greatly increase his own strength. This allows him to not only increase his arm to such enormous size that he can grab Kaidou's transformed body and yank him out of the Skull Dome , but also easily manhandle Kaidou despite his enormous size by swinging him around in circles and repeatedly slamming him on the ground. Luffy can even perform the inflation over his entire body at once to the point of him reaching the size of an actual giant . He can as well utilize his skillset from Gear 4 while using Gear 5, such as the limb compressions exhibited by Boundman for enhanced blows or the offensive properties of the defense utilized by Tankman . Unlike typical Zoan awakenings, Gear 5 has an ability akin to Paramecia awakenings: extending the user's rubbery nature to the environment around themselves. By making surfaces rubbery, Luffy can cushion himself from powerful blows and even bounce off or launch himself off of whatever surface his power affects. He can also stretch surfaces to bounce attacks back at his opponent. Interestingly, the altered terrain is seemingly more resilient than normal; a piece of the Skull Dome's roof was neither burnt nor melted while reflecting a Bolo Breath attack, which can melt such substances and has enough explosive power to destroy a castle and mountain top. Luffy has even applied this effect to intangible matter, such as air, lightning, and clouds, as well as to organic material. Although Luffy's punches cause his opponents' bodies to stretch around his fists, they still transfer the force of his attacks as well. In addition to the practical benefits of Gear 5's enhanced rubberiness and rubberizing, its cartoon-like freedom is emphasized further in Luffy's use of humorous props, such as morphing goggles from his flaming hair to punctuate the use of Dawn Rocket , and using paint and a baseball helmet to illustrate the baseball bat he chewed into shape from a tree trunk. It is still unclear as to if these items are an application of rubberization and an off-panel acquired prop, or if they are conjured by the fruit as a cosmetic illustration of the user's existing efforts. So far, these effects have not been of the kind to have an impact on the practical nature of the attack in question; the creation of the baseball bat to knock back Saturn's attack was produced by grabbing an existing tree and chewing it into shape, while the paint and helmet merely illustrate the baseball reference embedded in the action. However, while Luffy's reality bending power seems extremely powerful, it is nowhere near omnipotent. While he has shown the ability to manifest objects, they are, for the most part impermanent, disappearing a few seconds later. The alterations he does to the environment and other people are all also temporary, as even when he made the terrain rubbery, or flattened Saturn and Kizaru, they eventually regained their original form. Furthermore, despite the strength of this form, due to inexperience, it consumes plenty of energy for Luffy to maintain and use, even to the point that he can appear to have visibly aged decades once de-transformed in addition to experiencing extensive exhaustion and fatigue, although these effects will eventually wear off on their own after a while. In the anime, this is further emphasized with Luffy's colors becoming muted. That said, Luffy can fully restore his vitality and stamina right after using Gear 5 by eating excessive amounts of food. Alternatively, he can simply power through the taxing effects of Gear 5 through sheer willpower at the cost of extreme fatigue afterwards. Also, unlike Paramecia awakenings, Zoan awakenings like Gear 5 carries the inherent risk of the innate nature of the animal that the Gomu Gomu no Mi is derived from, namely the "Sun God" Nika, consuming Luffy's mind. In addition, Luffy is still vulnerable to cutting attacks. Techniques Techniques that are variations of Luffy's regular attacks tend to gain the label "Dawn" ( 白い , Dōn , literally meaning "White") after the "Gomu Gomu no" prefix in this form, for example Gomu Gomu no Dawn Rocket . The known named techniques that are used by Luffy so far in this form are as follows: Gomu Gomu no Fusen ( ゴムゴムの風船 , Gomu Gomu no Fūsen , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Balloon") : A variant of Luffy's standard technique, with the main difference being that Luffy's body becomes extremely buoyant, like a helium balloon. This was first used against Kaidou . In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Balloon . Gomu Gomu no Gigant ( ゴムゴムの 巨人 , Gomu Gomu no Giganto , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Giant") : A technique where Luffy expands himself to the size of a literal giant , as if activating Gear 3 on his entire body. This was first used against Kaidou. The name of the technique follows the Gear 3 naming theme, but without anything after Gigant . In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Giant . Gomu Gomu no Bajrang Gun ( ゴムゴムの 猿神銃 , Gomu Gomu no Bajurangu Gan , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Monkey God Gun") : The Gear 5 version of Gomu Gomu no King Kong Gun , and the most powerful technique Luffy has ever used in any form thus far. Luffy inflates his fist to almost the size of Onigashima , applying both advanced-grade Armament and Supreme King -infusion to it before unleashing it at his opponent—with the intention of outwardly emitting his in order to punch them without making direct contact—for a devastating blow. This was first used to defeat Kaidou. "Bajrang" is an alternate name of the Hindu monkey god, Hanuman , a potential inspiration for Sun Wukong . In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Bajrang Gun . Gomu Gomu no Mogura Pistol ( ゴムゴムのモグラ 銃 , Gomu Gomu no Mogura Pisutoru , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Mole Gun") : Luffy punches his fist into the floor, and a nearby section of the floor stretches into a tendril and strikes his opponent from the side. This was first seen being used against Rob Lucci . This is called Gum-Gum Mole Pistol in the VIZ manga and Crunchyroll dub and Gum-Gum Mole Pistols in the Crunchyroll subs. Gomu Gomu no Dawn Whip ( ゴムゴムの 白い 鞭 , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Wippu , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Whip") : The Gear 5 version of Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Muchi technique. After twisting his body, Luffy stretches his leg, hardens it with Armament , and swings it into his opponent's face. He then lets his body rapidly untwist, adding more energy into his kick. This was first seen being used against Rob Lucci. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Whip . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Rocket ( ゴムゴムの 白い ロケット , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Roketto , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Rocket") : The Gear 5 version of Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Rocket technique. Luffy throws himself into the environment and uses his awakened power to bend the structure further. After then shaping his hair into a pair of goggles, Luffy launches himself at his opponent with his fist spinning like a whip as he approaches and slams his fist into their abdomen by using the properties of fifth gear, the fist caricaturedly pierces the opponent's body. with enough force to send the opponent flying. This was first seen being used to defeat Rob Lucci. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Rocket . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Stamp ( ゴムゴムの 白い スタンプ , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Sutanpu , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Stamp") : The Gear 5 version of Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Stamp technique. Luffy lures a group of targets in a straight line, hardens his leg with Armament , and stretches it to attack them at once. This was first seen being used against Kizaru 's light clones . In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Stamp . Gomu Gomu no Star Gun ( ゴムゴムの 白星銃 , Gomu Gomu no Sutā Gan , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Star Gun") : After enlarging his muscles and hardening his arm with Armament and Supreme King , Luffy spins rapidly in place before stretching and delivers a powerful, enlarged hook to the opponent's head, which makes cartoon stars fly on impact. This was first seen being used against Kaidou , but went unnamed until it was done against Kizaru. In the anime, it begins with an Elephant Gun -sized fist that missed, prompting Luffy to spin around again, retracting the air from his fist and giving it an extra burst of speed. When used against Kizaru in the manga it was coated in flames, while in the anime it was covered in bright multicolored lights. It proved powerful enough to render Kizaru, a marine admiral , immobile. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Star Gun . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Gatling ( ゴムゴムの 白い銃乱打 , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Gatoringu , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Gun Battering") : The Gear 5 version of Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Elephant Gatling technique. With Gear 5 providing better control over his limb inflation, Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Elephant Gatling condition of needing to stretch his arms back, enlarge his fists and harden them with Armament beforehand is eliminated and can instead perform the setup at the same time he already executes his rapid barrage; thus, also eliminating the time lag of the initial technique. This was first seen being used against Jaygarcia Saturn . He can also perform the technique without enlarging his fists, as seen when he used it against Nerona Imu . In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Gatling . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Cymbal ( ゴムゴムの 白い 万力 , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Shinbaru , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Vise") : Luffy enlarges his hands and claps them together with his opponent in between, turning them into a flat disk that can be flung. This was first seen being used against Kizaru and Jaygarcia Saturn. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Cymbal . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Balloon ( ゴムゴムの 白い 風船 , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Barūn , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Balloon") : After inflating his body and covering it with Armament , Luffy grabs onto his target, pulls them into his belly, and then has his allies attack his back, compressing his body from both sides. Luffy then transfers the built-up force into his target, launching them a great distance through the air. This was first seen being used against Marcus Mars , with the help of Sanji 's Ifrit Jambe: Hell Memories , Franky 's Strong Impact Right , and Bonney 's Tokudai!! "Nika no Punch" . In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Balloon . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Pistol ( ゴムゴムの 白い 銃 , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Pisutoru , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Gun") : The Gear 5 version of Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Pistol technique. Luffy stretches his arm and delivers a punch at the opponent, now applying both Armament and Supreme King to his fist. This was first seen being used against Hilmungardr . In the VIZ manga, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Pistol . Gomu Gomu no Hara Senbei ( ゴムゴムの腹せんべい , Gomu Gomu no Hara Senbei ) : Luffy inflates his body and then spreads it out over a surface, forming a giant cushion. This was first seen being used to safely catch a group of falling giant children and their parents. In the VIZ manga, this is called Gum-Gum Tummy Cracker . Situational Techniques Gomu Gomu no Dasshutsu Rocket ( ゴムゴムの脱出ロケット , Gomu Gomu no Dasshutsu Roketto , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Escape Rocket") : A technique used by Luffy to escape from inside Kaidou's body after using Gomu Gomu no Fusen . Luffy stretches his arms through his opponent's eyes and grabs onto their nose to launch himself out. This was first used against Kaidou. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Escape Rocket . Gomu Gomu no Nawatobi ( ゴムゴムの縄跳び , Gomu Gomu no Nawatobi , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Skipping Rope") : The giant-sized Luffy grabs Kaidou in his dragon form and uses Kaidou's serpentine body as a skipping rope. This was first used against Kaidou. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Jump Rope . Gomu Gomu no Kaminari ( ゴムゴムの雷 , Gomu Gomu no Kaminari , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Lightning") : Luffy gives a lightning bolt the properties of rubber in order to grab it, then hurls it at his opponent. This was first used against Kaidou. In the anime Luffy was also shown to be able to grab and launch multiple bolts of lightning at once. In the English adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Lightning . Gomu Gomu no Dawn Thor Rifle ( ゴムゴムの 白い 雷 回転弾 , Gomu Gomu no Dōn Tōru Raifuru , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber White Lightning Rotating Bullet") : While in his giant form, Luffy twists his arm, grabs a lightning bolt, applies both Armament and Supreme King to his fist, and then delivers a punch that electrocutes the target. With his other hand, Luffy forms a circle with his thumb and forefinger and a creates a reticle within the circle, helping him aim his punch. This was first used against Shepherd Sommers . In the VIZ manga, this is called Gum-Gum Dawn Thor Rifle . Non-Canon Techniques Gomu Gomu no Okaeshi ( ゴムゴムのお返し , Gomu Gomu no Okaeshi , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Return Gift") : Luffy grabs onto a section of rubberized ground that has recoiled and stretched upwards from his opponent's attack, before bringing it over his head and then downwards towards his opponent to form a tall archway. He then bounces himself like a pinball down the section of ground, slamming himself into his target. This was first used against Kaidou. In the Funimation adaptations, this is called Gum-Gum Counterblow . Uchiyaburu Kaishin no Ichigeki ( 打ち破る会心の一撃 , Uchiyaburu Kaishin no Ichigeki , literally meaning "Enemy-Shattering Critical Strike") : Luffy charges up an attack reminiscent of Kong Gun, infuses it with advanced Armament and Supreme King , and releases the compressed fist in a punch, which has a red and orange lion-shaped aura. This was used alongside Shanks' attack to defeat Tot Musica . This is called Critical Strike Take Down in the English version of One Piece Treasure Cruise , and uses the placeholder name This is My Peak! in One Piece: Pirate Warriors 4 . Early Concepts A few of Oda's initial sketches for Gear 5 featured Luffy with a more sinister look. One featured black hair, similar to his previous Gears, while another seemed to have sharpened Luffy's fingers.

The Wara Wara no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to create and manipulate straw at will, with the straw having damage-redirecting properties. It was eaten by Basil Hawkins . Its powers were first demonstrated during the Sabaody Archipelago Arc , but it was not named until the Wano Country Arc . Etymology Wara ( 藁 ) is Japanese for "straw". Strengths and Weaknesses The fruit's main ability is the capacity to create and manipulate straw, which seems to have a broad variety of applications. At its most basic, the straw can serve as a holding device as well as act as sharp weapon. By producing a larger amount of straw, the user can cover themselves in it to enhance their fighting capabilities or create a large straw monster with which to attack their foes. When using these techniques, the user is also seemingly able to manifest nails and use them as accompanying weapons. As a more advanced technique, the user is also capable of creating small straw dolls and storing them inside their body. Each doll, through unknown means, can be linked to and represents another person akin to a voodoo doll. Consequently, if the user is injured in any way, the damage is instead transferred to a given doll and in turn whichever person it represents. This allows the user to easily survive and recover from multiple life-threatening injuries if they have enough dolls in store, making them difficult to defeat. It was also shown that effects that do not directly hurt or injure the user, such as the effects of the Ope Ope no Mi , can be transferred to other people as well. While the exact process is unclear, the user is capable of specifically choosing which person a straw doll is tied to. Thus, the user is capable of gaining a massive advantage in battle if they manage to connect their dolls to their opponents' allies, rendering the opponent unable to harm them without first killing one or more comrades. The user can further take advantage of tying an enemy's body to a doll by inflicting harm on themselves. In regard to the straw doll technique, the user can only carry a maximum of ten dolls in their body, leaving the user vulnerable to taking damage once every doll is lost. Moreover, if the person the doll represents suffers damage through other means, the doll is damaged too, leaving it unusable. Rapid consecutive attacks, in particular, can swiftly deplete an entire supply of Straw Men, as Kizaru proved. Additionally, if damage inflicted on the fruit user cannot be applied to the person the doll is connected to, the damage will not transfer and the user will be wounded. This was shown when Killer managed to sever Hawkins' left arm while the latter was transferring damage to Eustass Kid , as Kid had already lost his own left arm during the timeskip and used a prosthetic made with his own Devil Fruit abilities , meaning the damage transfer was rendered void. Moreover, the straw dolls can be removed manually from the user's body should an enemy somehow manage to get ahold of them, thus cancelling the link between the user and their target. The straw created by the fruit's power does not appear to be very durable, as Roronoa Zoro could easily slice through it. Aside from creating openings in their body to release the straw dolls, the user is incapable of turning their bodies into straw like a Logia , and thus cannot make attacks pass through them. Other than that, it has all the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Usually, Hawkins uses his fruit's powers in conjunction with his cartomancy and voodoo magic. When reading someone's fortune, he will sometimes summon thin straw threads to keep his cards at eye level. Post-timeskip, however, he has more or less dropped this practice, keeping his cards afloat in mid-air through unknown means instead. Hawkins tends to keep straw dolls representing other people inside of him, as a form of general protection against sudden enemies. These dolls can redirect a single instance of damage that he receives towards the person they are linked to, regenerating the corresponding damage that Hawkins took and leaving him practically unscathed as though it never even happened. How exactly Hawkins manages to link his life with those of others is still a mystery. Thanks to the nature of Hawkins' straw dolls, he can use the lives of people his enemies care about as a bargaining chip in order to attain an advantageous deal or circumstance. Hawkins also finds amusement in making his foes figure out ways to get around his powers, even if letting them unknowingly injure whomever he is linked to would be a more pragmatic and beneficial strategy. For offense, Hawkins can either transform into a large scarecrow-like creature with nail claws or summon an even larger straw entity to fight on his behalf. In both instances, the damage-redirecting effects of his ability remain active, diminishing the fact he creates a bigger or extra target to his own body. He may also turn his sword into straw for greater power and reach, using it akin to a whip. Techniques Straw Man ( 藁人形 , Sutorōman , literally meaning "Straw Doll") : Hawkins creates a small straw doll that represents another person and stores it within his body. These dolls are seemingly connected to both the person they represent and Hawkins himself, allowing him to use them to protect himself from injury and enemy attacks. Hawkins can store up to 10 dolls within his body at once, although he is capable of replenishing them after use. Hawkins first showcased this ability during his battle with Kizaru, though it was only named in his fight against Luffy and Zoro at Wano Country . He can also release his victims by removing those dolls from his body. In the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Strawman . "Life" Minus ( 「ライフ」マイナス , "Raifu" Mainasu ) : When Hawkins suffers an injury, the damage gets redirected to one of the dolls inside his body, and in turn to the person the doll represents, damaging them and leaving Hawkins unharmed. The doll is then expelled out of his body. Normally, each doll can only be used once, and the effect works in reverse too, meaning that if someone who has been marked by a straw doll receives damage through other means, the doll will receive the damage too, leaving it useless. This ability was first shown during his battle with Admiral Kizaru at Sabaody Archipelago, though it was only named in his fight against Luffy and Zoro at Wano Country. For uncommonly strong individuals, however, dolls fashioned after them can stay valid for as long as the damage is amenable, as seen when Hawkins deliberately and repeatedly injured himself to transfer the damage to Kid, crippling the latter in his fight against Big Mom. Goma no So ( 降魔の相 , Gōma no Sō , literally meaning "Devil Conquering Phase") : Hawkins covers his body with a large amount of straw, giving him the appearance of a giant scarecrow-like monster with metal nails serving as claws, which he can use to attack his enemies. He first used this technique in his battle against Kizaru. Despite his body becoming straw-like, it is noted that Hawkins is still vulnerable to attacks while in this form. The technique is called Demon Face in the VIZ Manga and Funimation dub, and Demon Conquering Phase in the Funimation subs. Straw Man's Card ( 藁人形 ズカード , Sutorōmanzu Kādo ) : By generating a large amount of straw from his sword, Hawkins creates a large straw monster that resembles his Goma no So technique. Once the entity detaches itself from his sword, Hawkins is able to remotely control it at will, with the Straw Man mimicking his movements. The Straw Man wields a variety of weapons, including a large spike-covered club, which it can swing with enormous strength (enough to make the superhumanly strong Zoro struggle to hold it back), and can also spit nails at its targets. However, the Straw Man's behavior is seemingly governed by what tarot cards Hawkins draws, which do not always have advantageous results. However, according to Hawkins, drawing bad cards tends to give better results for later draws. The cards have effects on his giant straw monster, those linked to him via straw dolls and his target(s). Since the Straw Man is linked to Hawkins's body, damaging it will also damage Hawkins himself, although he can still use his straw dolls to transfer the damage to someone else. This technique was first used during Hawkins's battle with Luffy and Zoro at Wano Country. In the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Strawman Cards . The Fool, Reversed ( 「愚か者」逆位置 , "Orokamono" Gyakuichi ) : The card of infighting. Causes those linked to Hawkins to attack each other. The Hierophant, Reversed ( 「法王」逆位置 , "Hōō" Gyakuichi ) : The card of pursuit. Prompts the straw monster to pursue its targets. The Hierophant ( 「法王」 , "Hōō" ) : The card of reinforcements. Indicates that his targets will escape with someone's help. Death, Upright ( 「死神」正位置 , "Shinigami" Seiichi ) : The card of destruction. Prompts the straw monster to strike its target with a scythe. The Tower ( 塔 , Tawā ) : The card symbolizing the collapse of the old with a hidden meaning of "a new path forward". It is unknown what effect this card entails on the straw monster, if any, since Hawkins was defeated by Killer before it could manifest again.
The user sends spiritual force through a target and damages it from the inside.
The user imbues a sword with spiritual force to increase cutting power.

Training to learn Observation Haki involves dodging various types of attacks while blindfolded, as well as counterattacking while blindfolded. The most important thing to remember is that every attack carries an intent, and a hint. One first trains by fighting against animals, and later against humans. Observation Haki is easier to use against animals because they always show their emotions and act on instinct, unlike humans who are capable of deception. set a goal for him: to dodge his attacks 100 times in a row. In some cases, an individual does not need to train in order to attain and use Observation Haki. Some people acquire it unconsciously within tense situations,

Roger and Whitebeard, two Supreme King Haki users who are among the most powerful people to ever live, clashed, the resulting impact blew back all of the flora and fauna on the entire island and even exerted tremendous force on their ships on the coast. 56 years ago, the resulting shockwave knocked away everyone within a 5 kilometer radius. Three Sword Style Two Sword Style One Sword Style No Sword Style Nine Sword Style Team Combinations Anime Only Video Game Only No Sword Style is a related fighting style that Zoro was forced to come up with during the game Groggy Ring event in the Davy Back Fight . Techniques This is an unorthodox form of his fighting style that uses no swords in which he was able to use his body's physical power to emit shockwaves resembling his sword slashes. Since he has rarely been in a situation without any swords, Zoro has only shown one technique with this fighting style: Tatsu Maki ( 龍巻き , Tatsu Maki , literally meaning "Dragon Twister") : The motions of this technique are the same as Three Sword Style's Tatsu Maki, only this version is performed without swords, only with the arms. Zoro's lack of swords means the technique has no cutting power, and is more used only to send the opponent flying. As with the sword version, the rotating motion of Zoro spinning himself creates a spinning vortex, which sends the opponent several feet skyward. However, getting hit by one of Zoro's arms as he performs this move could also send the shockwaves into the unlucky victim's body, causing great blunt trauma and harm, as a sort of substitute in place of the cutting power of the original version. This was first seen being used against Pickles during the Davy Back Fight with the Foxy Pirates . Like the Three Sword Style version, an image of a dragon appears behind Zoro when he uses this technique. In the VIZ manga and later Funimation subs, this is called Dragon Twister , while in the Funimation dub and initial subs, this technique keeps its original name. In the anime, Zoro used this technique again while in a gambling house in Wano . The following events are non-canon , and therefore not considered part of the canon story. Kusari Tatsu Maki ( 鎖 龍巻 , Kusari Tatsu Maki , literally meaning "Chain Dragon Twister") : A variation of No Sword Style's standard Tatsu Maki , used when Zoro is handcuffed to other people. Like the standard technique, Zoro rotates and creates a spinning vortex, this time with the added effect of hitting his target with the people he's chained to. It was used in One Piece Party to destroy a falling building while Zoro was chained to Luffy , Bartolomeo , and Cavendish . Ashura: Punch ( 阿修羅 パンチ , Ashura Panchi ) : A derivative of Kiki Kyutoryu: Ashura , used while fighting with No Sword Style. Zoro lunges forward, punching around him with all nine arms while doing so. The attack resembles Ashura: Ichibugin , only without swords. It was used in One Piece Party to stop a swarm of Germa 66 's clone soldiers . Concludes non-canon section.
